text.NRSV: Since many have undertaken to set down an orderly account of the events that have been fulfilled among us,
text.Rotherham: Seeing, indeed, that, many, had taken in hand to re-arrange for themselves a narrative, concerning the facts which have been fully confirmed amongst us,—

text.NRSV: just as they were handed on to us by those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word,
text.Rotherham: according as they who from the beginning became eye-witnesses and attendants of the Word delivered them unto us,

text.NRSV: I too decided, after investigating everything carefully from the very first, to write an orderly account for you, most excellent Theophilus,
text.Rotherham: it seemed good, even to me, having closely traced from the outset all things accurately, to write unto thee, in order, most excellent Theophilus:

text.NRSV: so that you may know the truth concerning the things about which you have been instructed.
text.Rotherham: that, as touching the matters which thou hadst been taught by word of mouth, thou mightest obtain full knowledge, of the certainty.

text.NRSV: In the days of King Herod of Judea, there was a priest named Zechariah, who belonged to the priestly order of Abijah. His wife was a descendant of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
text.Rotherham: It came to pass, in the days of Herod, king of Judaea, that there was a certain priest, by name Zachariah, of the daily course of Abia; and that he had a wife of the daughters of Aaron, and, her name, was Elizabeth.

text.NRSV: Both of them were righteous before God, living blamelessly according to all the commandments and regulations of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Now they were both righteous before God, walking in all the commandments and righteous appointments of the Lord, blameless;

text.NRSV: But they had no children, because Elizabeth was barren, and both were getting on in years.
text.Rotherham: and they had no child, inasmuch as Elizabeth was barren, and, both, had become, advanced in their days.

text.NRSV: Once when he was serving as priest before God and his section was on duty,
text.Rotherham: But it came to pass, as he was doing priestly service in the order of his daily course, before God,

text.NRSV: he was chosen by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to enter the sanctuary of the Lord and offer incense.
text.Rotherham: according to the custom of his priesthood, it fell to his lot to offer incense, entering into the Temple of the Lord;

text.NRSV: Now at the time of the incense offering, the whole assembly of the people was praying outside.
text.Rotherham: and, all the throng of the people, was praying outside, at the hour of the incense offering.

text.NRSV: Then there appeared to him an angel of the Lord, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.
text.Rotherham: And there appeared unto him a messenger of the Lord, standing on the right hand of the altar of incense;

text.NRSV: When Zechariah saw him, he was terrified; and fear overwhelmed him.
text.Rotherham: and Zachariah was troubled when he beheld, and, fear, fell upon him.

text.NRSV: But the angel said to him, “Do not be afraid, Zechariah, for your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will name him John.
text.Rotherham: But the messenger said unto him—Do not fear, Zachariah! Inasmuch as thy supplication hath been hearkened to,—and, thy wife Elizabeth, shall bring forth a son to thee, and thou shalt call his name, John;

text.NRSV: You will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth,
text.Rotherham: And there shall be joy to thee and exulting, and, many, over his birth, shall rejoice;

text.NRSV: for he will be great in the sight of the Lord. He must never drink wine or strong drink; even before his birth he will be filled with the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: For he shall be great before the Lord, and, wine and strong drink, in nowise may he drink, and, with Holy Spirit, shall he be filled, already, from his mother’s womb;

text.NRSV: He will turn many of the people of Israel to the Lord their God.
text.Rotherham: And, many of the Sons of Israel, shall he turn towards the Lord their God;

text.NRSV: With the spirit and power of Elijah he will go before him, to turn the hearts of parents to their children, and the disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous, to make ready a people prepared for the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, shall go before him, in the spirit and power of Elijah,—To turn the hearts of fathers unto children, and the unyielding, into the prudence of the righteous, and to prepare, for the Lord, a people made ready.

text.NRSV: Zechariah said to the angel, “How will I know that this is so? For I am an old man, and my wife is getting on in years.”
text.Rotherham: And Zachariah said unto the messenger—Whereby, shall I know this? for, I, am, aged, and, my wife, advanced in her days.

text.NRSV: The angel replied, “I am Gabriel. I stand in the presence of God, and I have been sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger, answering, said unto him—I, am Gabriel,—he that standeth near before God; and have been sent forth to speak unto thee, and to deliver the joyful message unto thee, as touching these things.

text.NRSV: But now, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their time, you will become mute, unable to speak, until the day these things occur.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! thou shalt be silent, and not able to speak until the day when these things shall come to pass; because thou didst not believe in my words,—the which shall be fulfilled for their season.

text.NRSV: Meanwhile the people were waiting for Zechariah, and wondered at his delay in the sanctuary.
text.Rotherham: And the people were expecting Zachariah, and began to marvel that he should tarry in the Temple;

text.NRSV: When he did come out, he could not speak to them, and they realized that he had seen a vision in the sanctuary. He kept motioning to them and remained unable to speak.
text.Rotherham: and when he came forth he was not able to speak unto them, and they perceived that, a vision, he had seen in the Temple,—and, he, continued making signs unto them, and remained dumb.

text.NRSV: When his time of service was ended, he went to his home.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the days of his public ministration were fulfilled, that he departed unto his house.

text.NRSV: After those days his wife Elizabeth conceived, and for five months she remained in seclusion. She said,
text.Rotherham: And, after these days, Elizabeth his wife conceived, and she disguised herself five months, saying—

text.NRSV: “This is what the Lord has done for me when he looked favorably on me and took away the disgrace I have endured among my people.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, for me, hath the Lord wrought,—in the days in which he looked upon me, to take away my reproach among men.

text.NRSV: In the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent by God to a town in Galilee called Nazareth,
text.Rotherham: Now, in the sixth month, was the messenger Gabriel sent forth from God, into a city of Galilee, the name of which was Nazareth,—

text.NRSV: to a virgin engaged to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David. The virgin’s name was Mary.
text.Rotherham: unto a virgin, betrothed to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David; and, the name of the virgin, was, Mary;

text.NRSV: And he came to her and said, “Greetings, favored one! The Lord is with you.”
text.Rotherham: and entering in unto her, he said—Joy to thee, favoured one! The Lord, be with thee!

text.NRSV: But she was much perplexed by his words and pondered what sort of greeting this might be.
text.Rotherham: And, she, at the word, was greatly troubled, and began to deliberate, of what kind, this salutation, might be.

text.NRSV: The angel said to her, “Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger said unto her—Do not fear, Mary, for thou hast found favour with God,—

text.NRSV: And now, you will conceive in your womb and bear a son, and you will name him Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And lo! thou shalt conceive in thy womb, and bring forth a son, and shalt call his name, Jesus:

text.NRSV: He will be great, and will be called the Son of the Most High, and the Lord God will give to him the throne of his ancestor David.
text.Rotherham: the same, shall be great, and, Son of the Most High, shall be called, and the Lord God, will give unto him, the throne of David his father,—

text.NRSV: He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and of his kingdom there will be no end.”
text.Rotherham: And he shall reign over the house of Jacob, unto the ages, and, of his kingdom, there shall be, no end.

text.NRSV: Mary said to the angel, “How can this be, since I am a virgin?”
text.Rotherham: But Mary said unto the messenger—How, shall this thing be, seeing that, a man, I know not?

text.NRSV: The angel said to her, “The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you; therefore the child to be born will be holy; he will be called Son of God.
text.Rotherham: And answering, the messenger said unto her—The Holy Spirit, shall come upon thee, and, the power of the Most High, shall overshadow thee; wherefore, even that which is to be born, Holy, shall be called, Son of God.

text.NRSV: And now, your relative Elizabeth in her old age has also conceived a son; and this is the sixth month for her who was said to be barren.
text.Rotherham: And lo! Elizabeth thy kinswoman, even she, hath conceived a son in, her old-age;—and, this month, is, the sixth, to her, the so-called barren one;

text.NRSV: For nothing will be impossible with God.”
text.Rotherham: Because no declaration from God, shall be void of power.

text.NRSV: Then Mary said, “Here am I, the servant of the Lord; let it be with me according to your word.” Then the angel departed from her.
text.Rotherham: And Mary said—Lo! the handmaid of the Lord! Might it come to pass unto me, according to thy declaration. And the messenger departed from her.

text.NRSV: In those days Mary set out and went with haste to a Judean town in the hill country,
text.Rotherham: And Mary, arising, in these days, journeyed into the hill country with haste, into a city of Judah,—

text.NRSV: where she entered the house of Zechariah and greeted Elizabeth.
text.Rotherham: and entered into the house of Zachariah, and saluted Elizabeth.

text.NRSV: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the child leaped in her womb. And Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, as Elizabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe leapt in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with Holy Spirit,—

text.NRSV: and exclaimed with a loud cry, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb.
text.Rotherham: and lifted up her voice with loud exclamation, and said—Blessed, art thou among women, and, blessed, is the fruit of thy womb;

text.NRSV: And why has this happened to me, that the mother of my Lord comes to me?
text.Rotherham: And, whence, to me is, this, That the mother of my Lord should come, unto me?

text.NRSV: For as soon as I heard the sound of your greeting, the child in my womb leaped for joy.
text.Rotherham: For lo! as the sound of thy salutation came into mine ears, the babe in my womb, leapt in exultation.

text.NRSV: And blessed is she who believed that there would be a fulfillment of what was spoken to her by the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And, happy, is she who hath believed, that there shall be a perfecting of the things which have been spoken to her from the Lord!

text.NRSV: And Mary said, “My soul magnifies the Lord,
text.Rotherham: And Mary said—My soul doth magnify the Lord,

text.NRSV: and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior,
text.Rotherham: And my spirit hath exulted upon God my saviour;

text.NRSV: for he has looked with favor on the lowliness of his servant. Surely, from now on all generations will call me blessed;
text.Rotherham: Because he hath looked upon the humbling of his handmaid; for lo! from the present time, all the generations, will pronounce me happy.

text.NRSV: for the Mighty One has done great things for me, and holy is his name.
text.Rotherham: Because he that is mighty hath done for me great things, and, holy, is his name;

text.NRSV: His mercy is for those who fear him from generation to generation.
text.Rotherham: And his mercy is unto generations and generations, to them who revere him;

text.NRSV: He has shown strength with his arm; he has scattered the proud in the thoughts of their hearts.
text.Rotherham: He hath wrought strength with his arm, He hath scattered men arrogant in the intention of their heart;

text.NRSV: He has brought down the powerful from their thrones, and lifted up the lowly;
text.Rotherham: He hath deposed potentates from thrones, and uplifted the lowly;

text.NRSV: he has filled the hungry with good things, and sent the rich away empty.
text.Rotherham: The hungry, hath he filled with good things, and, the wealthy, hath he sent empty away;

text.NRSV: He has helped his servant Israel, in remembrance of his mercy,
text.Rotherham: He hath laid hold of Israel his servant, to be mindful of mercies:

text.NRSV: according to the promise he made to our ancestors, to Abraham and to his descendants forever.”
text.Rotherham: According as he spake unto our fathers,—To Abraham, and to his seed,—Unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: And Mary remained with her about three months and then returned to her home.
text.Rotherham: And Mary abode with her about three months, and returned unto her house.

text.NRSV: Now the time came for Elizabeth to give birth, and she bore a son.
text.Rotherham: And, to Elizabeth, was the time fulfilled, that she should be bringing forth,—and she gave birth to a son.

text.NRSV: Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord had shown his great mercy to her, and they rejoiced with her.
text.Rotherham: And her neighbours and kinsfolk heard, that the Lord had magnified his mercy with her, and they were rejoicing with her.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day they came to baptize the child, and they were going to name him Zechariah after his father.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the eighth day, that they came to circumcise the child, and were calling it, after the name of its father, Zachariah.

text.NRSV: But his mother said, “No; he is to be called John.”
text.Rotherham: And his mother, answering, said—Nay! but he shall be called, John.

text.NRSV: They said to her, “None of your relatives has this name.”
text.Rotherham: And they said unto her—There is, no one from among thy kindred, who is called by this name!

text.NRSV: Then they began motioning to his father to find out what name he wanted to give him.
text.Rotherham: And they began making signs unto his father, as to what he might be wishing it to be called.

text.NRSV: He asked for a writing tablet and wrote, “His name is John.” And all of them were amazed.
text.Rotherham: And, asking for a small tablet, he wrote, saying—John, is his name! and they marvelled all.

text.NRSV: Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue freed, and he began to speak, praising God.
text.Rotherham: And his mouth was opened instantly, and his tongue [loosed], and he began to speak, blessing, God.

text.NRSV: Fear came over all their neighbors, and all these things were talked about throughout the entire hill country of Judea.
text.Rotherham: And fear came upon all the neighbours themselves; and, throughout all the hill-country of Judaea, were all these matters being much talked of;

text.NRSV: All who heard them pondered them and said, “What then will this child become?” For, indeed, the hand of the Lord was with him.
text.Rotherham: and all who heard laid [them] up in their hearts, saying—What then shall his child be? for, even the hand of the Lord, was with him.

text.NRSV: Then his father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke this prophecy:
text.Rotherham: And, Zachariah his father, was filled with Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying:—

text.NRSV: “Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he has looked favorably on his people and redeemed them.
text.Rotherham: Blessed, be the Lord, the God of Israel! Because he hath visited and wrought redemption for his people,

text.NRSV: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in the house of his servant David,
text.Rotherham: And hath raised up a horn of salvation for us, In the house of David his servant:

text.NRSV: as he spoke through the mouth of his holy prophets from of old,
text.Rotherham: According as he hath spoken by mouth of his holy ancient prophets,—

text.NRSV: that we would be saved from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us.
text.Rotherham: Of salvation from among our foes, and out of the hand of all them that hate us:

text.NRSV: Thus he has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and has remembered his holy covenant,
text.Rotherham: To perform mercy with our fathers, and to be mindful of his holy covenant,—

text.NRSV: the oath that he swore to our ancestor Abraham, to grant us
text.Rotherham: The oath which he sware unto Abraham our father, To grant us,

text.NRSV: that we, being rescued from the hands of our enemies, might serve him without fear,
text.Rotherham: without fear, from the hand of enemies rescued, to be rendering divine service unto him,

text.NRSV: in holiness and righteousness before him all our days.
text.Rotherham: in lovingkindness and righteousness before him, all our days.

text.NRSV: And you, child, will be called the prophet of the Most High; for you will go before the Lord to prepare his ways,
text.Rotherham: And, even thou, child, prophet of the Most High, shalt be called,—for thou shall march on before the Lord, to prepare his ways,

text.NRSV: to give knowledge of salvation to his people by the forgiveness of their sins.
text.Rotherham: Giving a knowledge of salvation unto his people, by a remission of their sins.

text.NRSV: By the tender mercy of our God, the dawn from on high will break upon us,
text.Rotherham: Because of the yearning compassion of the mercy of our God, wherein shall visit us a day-dawn from on high,—

text.NRSV: to give light to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.”
text.Rotherham: To shine on them who, in the darkness and shade of death, are sitting, to guide our feet into a way of peace.

text.NRSV: The child grew and became strong in spirit, and he was in the wilderness until the day he appeared publicly to Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, the child, went on growing, and being strengthened in spirit, and was in the deserts, until the day he was pointed out unto Israel.

text.NRSV: In those days a decree went out from Emperor Augustus that all the world should be registered.
text.Rotherham: Now it came to pass, in those days, that there went forth a decree from Caesar Augustus, for all the inhabited earth to be enrolled:

text.NRSV: This was the first registration and was taken while Quirinius was governor of Syria.
text.Rotherham: this enrolment, first was made while Cyrenius was governor of Syria:

text.NRSV: All went to their own towns to be registered.
text.Rotherham: and all were journeying to be enrolled, each one unto his own city.

text.NRSV: Joseph also went from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Judea, to the city of David called Bethlehem, because he was descended from the house and family of David.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of a city Nazareth, into Judaea, into the city of David which is called Bethlehem; because he was of the house and family of David:

text.NRSV: He went to be registered with Mary, to whom he was engaged and who was expecting a child.
text.Rotherham: to enrol himself with Mary who was betrothed to him, she being with child.

text.NRSV: While they were there, the time came for her to deliver her child.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while they were there, that the days were fulfilled for her to give birth;

text.NRSV: And she gave birth to her firstborn son and wrapped him in bands of cloth, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the inn.
text.Rotherham: and she gave birth to her firstborn son, and wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.

text.NRSV: In that region there were shepherds living in the fields, keeping watch over their flock by night.
text.Rotherham: And there were, shepherds, in the same country, dwelling in the fields, and keeping the watches of the night over their flock.

text.NRSV: Then an angel of the Lord stood before them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.
text.Rotherham: And, a messenger of the Lord, stood over them, and, a glory of the Lord, shone round about them; and they feared, a great fear.

text.NRSV: But the angel said to them, “Do not be afraid; for see—I am bringing you good news of great joy for all the people:
text.Rotherham: And the messenger said unto them—Be not afraid! For lo! I bring you good tidings of a great joy, the which shall be for all the people:

text.NRSV: to you is born this day in the city of David a Savior, who is the Messiah, the Lord.
text.Rotherham: That there hath been born unto you, this day, a saviour,—Who is Christ the Lord—in the city of David!

text.NRSV: This will be a sign for you: you will find a child wrapped in bands of cloth and lying in a manger.”
text.Rotherham: And, this, to you is, a sign: Ye shall find a babe, wrapped in swaddling clothes, and lying in a manger.

text.NRSV: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host, praising God and saying,
text.Rotherham: And, suddenly, there came to be, with the messenger, the throng of the heavenly host, praising God, and saying—

text.NRSV: “Glory to God in the highest heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors!”
text.Rotherham: Glory, in the highest, unto God! And, on earth, peace, among men of goodwill.

text.NRSV: When the angels had left them and gone into heaven, the shepherds said to one another, “Let us go now to Bethlehem and see this thing that has taken place, which the Lord has made known to us.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the messengers had departed from them into heaven, that, the shepherds, began to say one to another—Let us go through forthwith as far as to Bethlehem, and see this thing which hath come to pass, which the Lord hath made known to us.

text.NRSV: So they went with haste and found Mary and Joseph, and the child lying in the manger.
text.Rotherham: And they came, with haste, and found both Mary and Joseph, and the babe lying in the manger.

text.NRSV: When they saw this, they made known what had been told them about this child;
text.Rotherham: Now, when they beheld, they made known concerning the thing which had been told them as to this child.

text.NRSV: and all who heard it were amazed at what the shepherds told them.
text.Rotherham: And, all who heard, marvelled concerning the things which had been told by the shepherds unto them;

text.NRSV: But Mary treasured all these words and pondered them in her heart.
text.Rotherham: but, Mary, was closely observing, all, these things, putting them together in her heart.

text.NRSV: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen, as it had been told them.
text.Rotherham: And the shepherds returned, giving glory and singing praise unto God, over all things which they had heard and seen, according as it had been told unto them.

text.NRSV: After eight days had passed, it was time to circumcise the child; and he was called Jesus, the name given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
text.Rotherham: And, when eight days were fulfilled for circumcising him, then was his name called, Jesus,—which it was called by the messenger, before he was conceived in the womb.

text.NRSV: When the time came for their purification according to the law of Moses, they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord
text.Rotherham: And, when the days of their purification, according to the law of Moses, were fulfilled, they took him up into Jerusalem, to present [him] unto the Lord,—

text.NRSV: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every firstborn male shall be designated as holy to the Lord”),
text.Rotherham: according as it is written in the law of the Lord—Every male that is a firstborn, Holy unto the Lord, shall be called,

text.NRSV: and they offered a sacrifice according to what is stated in the law of the Lord, “a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons.”
text.Rotherham: and to give a sacrifice, according to that which is said in the law of the Lord—A pair of turtle-doves, or two young pigeons.

text.NRSV: Now there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon; this man was righteous and devout, looking forward to the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
text.Rotherham: And lo! there was, a man, in Jerusalem, whose name, was Symeon; and, this man, was righteous and devout, awaiting the consolation of Israel, and Holy Spirit was upon him;

text.NRSV: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Messiah.
text.Rotherham: and it had been intimated to him by the Holy Spirit, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Christ of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Guided by the Spirit, Simeon came into the temple; and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him what was customary under the law,
text.Rotherham: And he came, in the Spirit, into the temple; and, when the parents brought in the child Jesus, that they might do according to that which was customary by the law concerning it,

text.NRSV: Simeon took him in his arms and praised God, saying,
text.Rotherham: even he, welcomed it into his arms, and blessed God, and said—

text.NRSV: “Master, now you are dismissing your servant in peace, according to your word;
text.Rotherham: Now, dost thou dismiss thy servant, O Sovereign, according to thy declaration—in peace;

text.NRSV: for my eyes have seen your salvation,
text.Rotherham: Because mine eyes have seen thy salvation,

text.NRSV: which you have prepared in the presence of all peoples,
text.Rotherham: Which thou hast prepared in face of all the peoples:

text.NRSV: a light for revelation to the Gentiles and for glory to your people Israel.”
text.Rotherham: A light for the unveiling of nations, and the glory of thy people Israel.

text.NRSV: And the child’s father and mother were amazed at what was being said about him.
text.Rotherham: And his father and mother were marvelling over the things which were being spoken concerning him.

text.NRSV: Then Simeon blessed them and said to his mother Mary, “This child is destined for the falling and the rising of many in Israel, and to be a sign that will be opposed
text.Rotherham: And Symeon blessed them, and said unto Mary his mother—Lo! this one, is being set for the falling and rising of many in Israel, and for a sign to be spoken against;

text.NRSV: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed—and a sword will pierce your own soul too.”
text.Rotherham: And, thou! through thy very soul, shall pass a sword, that, reasonings, may be revealed, out of many hearts.

text.NRSV: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was of a great age, having lived with her husband seven years after her marriage,
text.Rotherham: And there was one Anna, a prophetess, daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher;—the same, advanced in many days; having lived with a husband seven years from her virginity,

text.NRSV: then as a widow to the age of eighty-four. She never left the temple but worshiped there with fasting and prayer night and day.
text.Rotherham: and, she, had been a widow for as long as eighty-four years,—who left not the temple, with fastings and supplications, rendering divine service night and day;—

text.NRSV: At that moment she came, and began to praise God and to speak about the child to all who were looking for the redemption of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: and, in that very hour, coming near, she began to give thanks unto God, and to speak concerning him unto all who were waiting for the redemption of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: When they had finished everything required by the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own town of Nazareth.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had finished all things that were according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, into their own city, Nazareth.

text.NRSV: The child grew and became strong, filled with wisdom; and the favor of God was upon him.
text.Rotherham: And, the child, went on growing, and waxing strong, becoming filled with wisdom; and, the favour of God, was upon it.

text.NRSV: Now every year his parents went to Jerusalem for the festival of the Passover.
text.Rotherham: And his parents used to journey yearly into Jerusalem, at the feast of the passover.

text.NRSV: But now more than ever the word about Jesus spread abroad; many crowds would gather to hear him and to be cured of their diseases.
text.Rotherham: But the report concerning him the more went abroad, and many multitudes were coming together, to hear, and be getting cured from their infirmities;

text.NRSV: But he would withdraw to deserted places and pray.
text.Rotherham: howbeit, he, was retiring in the deserts, and engaging in prayer.

text.NRSV: One day, while he was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the law were sitting near by (they had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and from Jerusalem); and the power of the Lord was with him to heal.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on one of the days, that, he, was teaching, and there were sitting Pharisees and Teachers of the law, who had come out of every village of Galilee and Judaea and Jerusalem; and, the power of the Lord, was there, that he might heal.

text.NRSV: Just then some men came, carrying a paralyzed man on a bed. They were trying to bring him in and lay him before Jesus;
text.Rotherham: And lo! men bearing, upon a couch, one who was paralyzed, and they were seeking to bring him in, and lay him before him.

text.NRSV: but finding no way to bring him in because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and let him down with his bed through the tiles into the middle of the crowd in front of Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, not finding by what means they might bring him in, because of the multitude, going up on the house-top, through the tiling, let they him down, with the little-couch, into the midst before Jesus.

text.NRSV: When he saw their faith, he said, “Friend, your sins are forgiven you.”
text.Rotherham: And, beholding their faith, he said—O man! thy sins are forgiven thee.

text.NRSV: Then the scribes and the Pharisees began to question, “Who is this who is speaking blasphemies? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
text.Rotherham: And the Scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying—Who is this that speaketh profanities? Who can forgive, sins, save, God alone?

text.NRSV: When Jesus perceived their questionings, he answered them, “Why do you raise such questions in your hearts?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, taking note of their reasonings, answering, said unto them—Why are ye reasoning in your hearts?

text.NRSV: Which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you,’ or to say, ‘Stand up and walk’?
text.Rotherham: Which is easier, To say—Thy sins are forgiven thee; or to say—Arise and be walking?

text.NRSV: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the one who was paralyzed—“I say to you, stand up and take your bed and go to your home.”
text.Rotherham: But, that ye may know that, the Son of Man, hath, authority, upon the earth to forgive sins—he said to the paralyzed man—To thee, I say, Arise, and, taking up thy couch, be going thy way unto thy house.

text.NRSV: Immediately he stood up before them, took what he had been lying on, and went to his home, glorifying God.
text.Rotherham: And, instantly arising before them, he took up that whereon he had been lying, and departed unto his house, glorifying God.

text.NRSV: Amazement seized all of them, and they glorified God and were filled with awe, saying, “We have seen strange things today.”
text.Rotherham: And, astonishment, seized one and all, and they began glorifying God, and were filled with fear, saying—We have seen unaccountable things, to-day!

text.NRSV: After this he went out and saw a tax collector named Levi, sitting at the tax booth; and he said to him, “Follow me.”
text.Rotherham: And, after these things, he went forth, and looked upon a tax-collector, by name Levi,—presiding over the tax-office; and he said to him—Be following me!

text.NRSV: And he got up, left everything, and followed him.
text.Rotherham: And, forsaking all, he arose, and was following him.

text.NRSV: Then Levi gave a great banquet for him in his house; and there was a large crowd of tax collectors and others sitting at the table with them.
text.Rotherham: And Levi made a great reception for him, in his house, and there was a great multitude of tax-collectors, and others,—who were with them reclining.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees and their scribes were complaining to his disciples, saying, “Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?”
text.Rotherham: And the Pharisees and their Scribes began murmuring unto his disciples, saying—Wherefore, with the tax-collectors and sinners, are ye eating and drinking?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick;
text.Rotherham: And, answering, Jesus said unto them—No need, have, the whole, of a physician, but, they who are sick.

text.NRSV: I have come to call not the righteous but sinners to repentance.”
text.Rotherham: I have not come to call, righteous, men, but, sinners, unto repentance.

text.NRSV: Then they said to him, “John’s disciples, like the disciples of the Pharisees, frequently fast and pray, but your disciples eat and drink.”
text.Rotherham: But, they, said unto him—The disciples of John, do fast much, and, supplications, do make,—likewise also the disciples of the Pharisees; but, thine, do eat and drink!

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “You cannot make wedding guests fast while the bridegroom is with them, can you?
text.Rotherham: Jesus, however, said unto them—Can, ye make, the sons of the bridechamber, fast, while the bridegroom is with them?

text.NRSV: The days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast in those days.”
text.Rotherham: But there will come days, even when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, then, will they fast, in those days.

text.NRSV: He also told them a parable: “No one tears a piece from a new garment and sews it on an old garment; otherwise the new will be torn, and the piece from the new will not match the old.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, he went on to speak, a parable also, unto them—No one, rending a patch from a new mantle, patcheth it upon an old mantle; otherwise, at least, both, the new, he will rend, and, with the old, the patch which is from the new, will not agree.

text.NRSV: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the new wine will burst the skins and will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.
text.Rotherham: And, no one, poureth new wine into old skins; otherwise, at least, the new wine, will burst the skins, and will, itself, be poured out, and the skins be destroyed.

text.NRSV: But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins.
text.Rotherham: But, new wine, into unused skins, must be poured.

text.NRSV: And no one after drinking old wine desires new wine, but says, ‘The old is good.’”
text.Rotherham: [No one, having drunk old, desireth new; for he saith, The old, is, mellow.]

text.NRSV: One sabbath while Jesus was going through the grainfields, his disciples plucked some heads of grain, rubbed them in their hands, and ate them.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on a Sabbath, that he was passing along through cornfields, and his disciples were plucking and eating the ears of corn, rubbing them with their hands.

text.NRSV: But some of the Pharisees said, “Why are you doing what is not lawful on the sabbath?”
text.Rotherham: But, certain of the Pharisees, said—Why are ye doing what is not allowed on the Sabbath?

text.NRSV: Jesus answered, “Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
text.Rotherham: And, making answer unto them, Jesus said—Have ye never read, even this, what David did when he hungered, he, and they who were with him—

text.NRSV: He entered the house of God and took and ate the bread of the Presence, which it is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and gave some to his companions?”
text.Rotherham: How he entered into the house of God, and, the presence-bread, receiving, did eat, and gave to them who were with him, which it is not allowable to eat, save alone, to the priests?

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “The Son of Man is lord of the sabbath.”
text.Rotherham: And he was saying to them—The Son of Man is, Lord of the Sabbath.

text.NRSV: On another sabbath he entered the synagogue and taught, and there was a man there whose right hand was withered.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on another Sabbath, that he entered into the synagogue, and was teaching, and there was a man there, and, his right hand, was withered.

text.NRSV: The scribes and the Pharisees watched him to see whether he would cure on the sabbath, so that they might find an accusation against him.
text.Rotherham: Now the Scribes and the Pharisees were narrowly watching him, whether, on the Sabbath, he healeth,—that they might find whereof to accuse him.

text.NRSV: Even though he knew what they were thinking, he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Come and stand here.” He got up and stood there.
text.Rotherham: But, he, knew their reasonings, and said to the man who had the, withered, hand—Arise, and stand forward in the midst! And, arising, he stood forward.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “I ask you, is it lawful to do good or to do harm on the sabbath, to save life or to destroy it?”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto them—I ask you, whether it is allowed, on the Sabbath, to do good or to do harm,—life, to save, or, to destroy.

text.NRSV: After looking around at all of them, he said to him, “Stretch out your hand.” He did so, and his hand was restored.
text.Rotherham: And, looking round upon them all, he said unto him—Stretch forth thy hand! and, he, did so, and his hand was restored.

text.NRSV: But they were filled with fury and discussed with one another what they might do to Jesus.
text.Rotherham: But, they, were filled with folly, and began conversing one with another, as to, what they might do with Jesus.

text.NRSV: Now during those days he went out to the mountain to pray; and he spent the night in prayer to God.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in these days, that he went forth into the mountain to pray, and was spending the night in the prayer-house of God.

text.NRSV: And when day came, he called his disciples and chose twelve of them, whom he also named apostles:
text.Rotherham: And, when it became day, he called his disciples, and chose from them, twelve, whom also he named, Apostles,—

text.NRSV: Simon, whom he named Peter, and his brother Andrew, and James, and John, and Philip, and Bartholomew,
text.Rotherham: Simon, whom also he named Peter, and Andrew his brother; and James and John; and Philip and Bartholomew;

text.NRSV: and Matthew, and Thomas, and James son of Alphaeus, and Simon, who was called the Zealot,
text.Rotherham: and Matthew and Thomas; and James, son of Alphaeus, and Simon, who was called Zealot; and Judas, [son] of James,

text.NRSV: and Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
text.Rotherham: and Judas Iscariot, who became betrayer;—

text.NRSV: He came down with them and stood on a level place, with a great crowd of his disciples and a great multitude of people from all Judea, Jerusalem, and the coast of Tyre and Sidon.
text.Rotherham: and, coming down with them, he stood upon a level place, also a great multitude of his disciples,—and a great throng of the people, from all Judaea and Jerusalem and the sea-coast of Tyre and Zidon, who had come to hearken unto him and to be healed from their diseases;

text.NRSV: They had come to hear him and to be healed of their diseases; and those who were troubled with unclean spirits were cured.
text.Rotherham: and, they who were molested by impure spirits, were being cured;

text.NRSV: And all in the crowd were trying to touch him, for power came out from him and healed all of them.
text.Rotherham: and, all the multitude, were seeking to touch him, because, power, from him, was coming forth, and healing all.

text.NRSV: Then he looked up at his disciples and said: “Blessed are you who are poor, for yours is the kingdom of God.
text.Rotherham: And, he, lifting up his eyes towards his disciples, was saying:—Happy, ye destitute, for, yours, is the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you who are hungry now, for you will be filled. “Blessed are you who weep now, for you will laugh.
text.Rotherham: Happy, ye that hunger now, for ye shall be filled. Happy, ye that weep now, for ye shall laugh.

text.NRSV: “Blessed are you when people hate you, and when they exclude you, revile you, and defame you on account of the Son of Man.
text.Rotherham: Happy, are ye, whensoever men shall hate you, and whensoever they shall separate you, and reproach you, and cast out your name as evil, for the sake of the Son of Man:

text.NRSV: Rejoice on that day and leap for joy, for surely your reward is great in heaven; for that is what their ancestors did to the prophets.
text.Rotherham: be rejoiced in that day, and leap, for lo! your reward, is great in heaven; for, according to the same things, were their fathers doing unto the prophets.

text.NRSV: “But woe to you who are rich, for you have received your consolation.
text.Rotherham: But alas! for you, ye wealthy, for ye are duly receiving you consolation.

text.NRSV: “Woe to you who are full now, for you will be hungry. “Woe to you who are laughing now, for you will mourn and weep.
text.Rotherham: Alas! for you, ye who are filled full now, for ye shall hunger. Alas! ye that laugh now, for ye shall mourn and weep.

text.NRSV: “Woe to you when all speak well of you, for that is what their ancestors did to the false prophets.
text.Rotherham: Alas! whensoever all men shall, speak well of you, for, according to the same things, were their fathers doing unto the false prophets.

text.NRSV: “But I say to you that listen, Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,
text.Rotherham: But, unto you, I say, who are hearkening: Be loving your enemies; be doing, good, unto them that hate you;

text.NRSV: bless those who curse you, pray for those who abuse you.
text.Rotherham: Be blessing them that curse you; be praying for them that wantonly insult you.

text.NRSV: If anyone strikes you on the cheek, offer the other also; and from anyone who takes away your coat do not withhold even your shirt.
text.Rotherham: To him who is smiting thee upon the one cheek, be offering, the other also; and, from him who is taking away thy mantle, thy tunic also, do not forbid:

text.NRSV: Give to everyone who begs from you; and if anyone takes away your goods, do not ask for them again.
text.Rotherham: To every one asking thee, give, and, from him that taketh away thy possessions, ask them not back.

text.NRSV: Do to others as you would have them do to you.
text.Rotherham: And, according as ye desire that men be doing unto you, be ye doing unto them, in like manner.

text.NRSV: “If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners love those who love them.
text.Rotherham: And, if ye love them that love you, what sort of thanks are there for you? for, even sinners, love, such as love them.

text.NRSV: If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners do the same.
text.Rotherham: [For], if ye even do good unto them that do good unto you, what sort of thanks are there for you? Even sinners, the same, are doing.

text.NRSV: If you lend to those from whom you hope to receive, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, to receive as much again.
text.Rotherham: And, if ye lend to them from whom ye are hoping to receive, what sort of thanks are there for you? Even sinners, unto sinners, do lend, that they may receive back, as much.

text.NRSV: But love your enemies, do good, and lend, expecting nothing in return. Your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High; for he is kind to the ungrateful and the wicked.
text.Rotherham: But love your enemies, and do good and lend, hoping for, nothing, back; and your reward shall be, great, and ye shall be sons of the Most High,—for, he, is, gracious, unto the ungrateful and wicked.

text.NRSV: Be merciful, just as your Father is merciful.
text.Rotherham: Become ye compassionate, according as, your Father, is, compassionate;

text.NRSV: “Do not judge, and you will not be judged; do not condemn, and you will not be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven;
text.Rotherham: And do not judge, and in nowise shall ye be judged; and do not condemn, and in nowise shall ye be condemned; release, and ye shall be released;

text.NRSV: give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together, running over, will be put into your lap; for the measure you give will be the measure you get back.”
text.Rotherham: Give, and it shall be given unto you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, running over, will they give into your lap; for, with what measure ye mete, shall it be measured back unto you.

text.NRSV: He also told them a parable: “Can a blind person guide a blind person? Will not both fall into a pit?
text.Rotherham: He spake, moreover, a parable also, unto them: Can, the blind, guide, the blind? will not, both, fall into, a ditch?

text.NRSV: A disciple is not above the teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will be like the teacher.
text.Rotherham: A disciple is not above the teacher; but, when trained, every one shall be as his teacher.

text.NRSV: Why do you see the speck in your neighbor’s eye, but do not notice the log in your own eye?
text.Rotherham: But why beholdest thou the mote that is in the eye of thy brother, while, the beam that is in thine own eye, thou dost not consider?

text.NRSV: Or how can you say to your neighbor, ‘Friend, let me take out the speck in your eye,’ when you yourself do not see the log in your own eye? You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your neighbor’s eye.
text.Rotherham: How canst thou say to thy brother—Brother! let me cast out the mote that is in thine eye,—thyself, the beam in thine own eye, not beholding? Hypocrite! cast out, first, the beam out of thine own eye, and, then, shalt thou see clearly, to cast out, the mote that is in the eye of thy brother.

text.NRSV: “No good tree bears bad fruit, nor again does a bad tree bear good fruit;
text.Rotherham: For a good tree doth not produce worthless fruit, neither again doth, a worthless tree, produce good fruit.

text.NRSV: for each tree is known by its own fruit. Figs are not gathered from thorns, nor are grapes picked from a bramble bush.
text.Rotherham: For, every tree, by its own fruit, becometh known. For not, of thorns, do they gather figs, neither, of a bramble-bush, do they gather, a bunch of grapes.

text.NRSV: The good person out of the good treasure of the heart produces good, and the evil person out of evil treasure produces evil; for it is out of the abundance of the heart that the mouth speaks.
text.Rotherham: The good man, out of the good treasure of the heart, bringeth forth that which is good; and, the wicked man, out of the wicked heart, bringeth forth that which is wicked; for, out of an overflowing of heart, speaketh, his mouth.

text.NRSV: “Why do you call me ‘Lord, Lord,’ and do not do what I tell you?
text.Rotherham: And why call ye me, Lord! Lord! and not do the things that I say?

text.NRSV: I will show you what someone is like who comes to me, hears my words, and acts on them.
text.Rotherham: Every one coming unto me, and hearkening unto my words, and doing them, I will suggest to you, whom he is like:

text.NRSV: That person is like a man building a house, who dug deeply and laid the foundation on rock; when a flood arose, the river burst against that house but could not shake it, because it had been well built.
text.Rotherham: He is like unto a man building a house, who digged, and deepened, and laid a foundation upon the rock,—and, a flood, coming, the stream burst against that house, and was not strong enough to shake it, because it had been, well, built.

text.NRSV: But the one who hears and does not act is like a man who built a house on the ground without a foundation. When the river burst against it, immediately it fell, and great was the ruin of that house.”
text.Rotherham: But, he that hath heard and not done, is like unto a man having built a house upon the earth, without a foundation,—against which the stream burst, and, straightway, it fell in; and it came to pass, that, the crash of that house, was, great.

text.NRSV: After Jesus had finished all his sayings in the hearing of the people, he entered Capernaum.
text.Rotherham: After he had completed all his sayings within the hearing of the people, he entered into Capernaum.

text.NRSV: A centurion there had a servant whom he valued highly, and who was ill and close to death.
text.Rotherham: And, a certain centurion’s servant, being sick, was on the point of dying,—who was, by him, highly prized.

text.NRSV: When he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to him, asking him to come and heal his servant.
text.Rotherham: But, hearing about Jesus, he sent forth unto him elders of the Jews, requesting him that he would come and bring his servant safely through.

text.NRSV: When they came to Jesus, they appealed to him earnestly, saying, “He is worthy of having you do this for him,
text.Rotherham: And, they, coming near unto Jesus, began beseeching him earnestly, saying—Worthy, is he for whom thou mayest do this,

text.NRSV: for he loves our people, and it is he who built our synagogue for us.”
text.Rotherham: for he loveth our nation, and, the synagogue, he, built for us.

text.NRSV: And Jesus went with them, but when he was not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to say to him, “Lord, do not trouble yourself, for I am not worthy to have you come under my roof;
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, was journeying with them. But, he, by this time, being not far from the house, the centurion sent friends, saying unto him—Lord! do not trouble thyself, for I am of no consideration, that, under my roof, thou shouldst enter.

text.NRSV: therefore I did not presume to come to you. But only speak the word, and let my servant be healed.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, neither deemed I, myself, worthy to come unto thee,—but speak with a word, and let my servant be healed.

text.NRSV: For I also am a man set under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes, and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes, and to my slave, ‘Do this,’ and the slave does it.”
text.Rotherham: For, I too, am a man ranked, under authority, having, under myself, soldiers; and I say to this one—Go! and he goeth,—and to another—Come! and he cometh,—and to my servant—Do this! and he doeth it.

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard this he was amazed at him, and turning to the crowd that followed him, he said, “I tell you, not even in Israel have I found such faith.”
text.Rotherham: And, hearing these things, Jesus marvelled at him; and, turning to the multitude following him, said—I tell you, Not even in Israel, such faith as this, have I found!

text.NRSV: When those who had been sent returned to the house, they found the servant in good health.
text.Rotherham: And they who were sent, returning unto the house, found the servant, well.

text.NRSV: Soon afterwards he went to a town called Nain, and his disciples and a large crowd went with him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass thereafter, that he journeyed unto a city called Nain, and there were journeying with him, his disciples and a great multitude.

text.NRSV: As he approached the gate of the town, a man who had died was being carried out. He was his mother’s only son, and she was a widow; and with her was a large crowd from the town.
text.Rotherham: Now, as he drew near unto the gate of the city, then lo! there was being brought forth, one dead, the only-begotten son of his mother,—and, she, was a widow; and, a considerable multitude of the city, was with her.

text.NRSV: When the Lord saw her, he had compassion for her and said to her, “Do not weep.”
text.Rotherham: And, beholding her, the Lord, was moved with compassion over her, and said to her—Be not weeping!

text.NRSV: Then he came forward and touched the bier, and the bearers stood still. And he said, “Young man, I say to you, rise!”
text.Rotherham: And, going forward, he touched the coffin, and the bearers stood still; and he said—Young man! to thee, I say, Arise!

text.NRSV: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
text.Rotherham: And the dead man sat up, and began to speak; and he gave him to his mother.

text.NRSV: Fear seized all of them, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has risen among us!” and “God has looked favorably on his people!”
text.Rotherham: And fear seized them, all, and they began to glorify God, saying—A great prophet, hath been raised up amongst us! and—God hath visited his people!

text.NRSV: This word about him spread throughout Judea and all the surrounding country.
text.Rotherham: And this report went forth throughout the whole of Judaea, and all the surrounding country.

text.NRSV: The disciples of John reported all these things to him. So John summoned two of his disciples
text.Rotherham: And John’s disciples carried tidings unto him concerning all these things.

text.NRSV: and sent them to the Lord to ask, “Are you the one who is to come, or are we to wait for another?”
text.Rotherham: And, calling unto him certain two of his disciples, John sent unto the Lord, saying—Art, thou, the Coming One, or, a different one, are we to expect?

text.NRSV: When the men had come to him, they said, “John the Baptist has sent us to you to ask, ‘Are you the one who is to come, or are we to wait for another?’”
text.Rotherham: And, coming near unto him, the men said—John the Immerser, hath sent us unto thee, saying, Art, thou, the Coming One, or, another, are we to expect?

text.NRSV: Jesus had just then cured many people of diseases, plagues, and evil spirits, and had given sight to many who were blind.
text.Rotherham: In that very hour, he cured many from diseases, and plagues, and wicked spirits; and, unto many blind, gave he the favour to see.

text.NRSV: And he answered them, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard: the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, the poor have good news brought to them.
text.Rotherham: And, answering, he said unto them—Go and bear tidings unto John, as to what ye have seen and heard: The blind, are receiving sight, the lame, walk, lepers, are cleansed, and, the deaf, hear, the dead, are raised, the destitute, are told the glad-message;

text.NRSV: And blessed is anyone who takes no offense at me.”
text.Rotherham: And, happy, is he, whosoever shall not find occasion of stumbling in me!

text.NRSV: When John’s messengers had gone, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to look at? A reed shaken by the wind?
text.Rotherham: And, the messengers of John having departed, he began to say unto the multitudes, concerning John—What went ye forth into the desert to gaze at? A reed, by a wind, shaken?

text.NRSV: What then did you go out to see? Someone dressed in soft robes? Look, those who put on fine clothing and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
text.Rotherham: But what went ye forth to see? A man, in soft garments, arrayed? Lo! they who in splendid apparel and luxury are found, are, in the kingly courts.

text.NRSV: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
text.Rotherham: But what went ye forth to see? A prophet? Yea! I say unto you,—and abundantly more than a prophet.

text.NRSV: This is the one about whom it is written, ‘See, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way before you.’
text.Rotherham: This, is he concerning whom it is written—Lo! I am sending forth my messenger before thy face, who shall prepare thy way before thee.

text.NRSV: I tell you, among those born of women no one is greater than John; yet the least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.”
text.Rotherham: I say unto you—A greater, among them that are born of women, than John, is there none; but, he that is least in the kingdom of God, is greater than he.

text.NRSV: (And all the people who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged the justice of God, because they had been baptized with John’s baptism;
text.Rotherham: And, all the people, when they heard, and the tax-collectors, justified God, having been immersed with the immersion of John;

text.NRSV: but by refusing to be baptized by him, the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected God’s purpose for themselves.)
text.Rotherham: but, the Pharisees and the lawyers, had set aside, the counsel of God against themselves, not being immersed by him.

text.NRSV: “To what then will I compare the people of this generation, and what are they like?
text.Rotherham: Unto what, then, shall I liken the men of this generation? And unto what are they like?

text.NRSV: They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling to one another, ‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we wailed, and you did not weep.’
text.Rotherham: They are like unto the children sitting, in a market-place, and calling one to another, who say—We played the flute for you, and ye danced not, We wailed, and ye wept not.

text.NRSV: For John the Baptist has come eating no bread and drinking no wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon’;
text.Rotherham: For John the Immerser hath come, neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and ye say—A demon, he hath:

text.NRSV: the Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Look, a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’
text.Rotherham: The Son of Man hath come, eating and drinking, and ye say—Lo! a man, gluttonous and a wine-drinker, a friend of tax-collectors and sinners.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless, wisdom is vindicated by all her children.”
text.Rotherham: And yet wisdom hath been justified by all her children.

text.NRSV: One of the Pharisees asked Jesus to eat with him, and he went into the Pharisee’s house and took his place at the table.
text.Rotherham: And a certain one of the Pharisees was requesting him, that he would eat with him; and, entering into the house of the Pharisee, he reclined.

text.NRSV: And a woman in the city, who was a sinner, having learned that he was eating in the Pharisee’s house, brought an alabaster jar of ointment.
text.Rotherham: And, lo! a woman, who indeed was in the city a sinner; and, when she found out that he was reclining in the house of the Pharisee, providing an alabaster-jar of perfume,

text.NRSV: She stood behind him at his feet, weeping, and began to bathe his feet with her tears and to dry them with her hair. Then she continued kissing his feet and anointing them with the ointment.
text.Rotherham: and standing behind, near his feet, weeping, with the tears, began she to be wetting his feet, and, with the hair of her head, was wiping off [the tears], and was tenderly kissing his feet, and anointing them with the perfume.

text.NRSV: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he said to himself, “If this man were a prophet, he would have known who and what kind of woman this is who is touching him—that she is a sinner.”
text.Rotherham: But the Pharisee who had invited him, seeing it, spake within himself, saying, This one, if he were the prophet, would have been taking note, who and of what sort, is the woman, who is even touching him, that she is, a sinner.

text.NRSV: Jesus spoke up and said to him, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” “Teacher,” he replied, “Speak.”
text.Rotherham: And, making answer, Jesus said unto him—Simon! I have, unto thee, something to say. He, then—Teacher, speak!—saith he.

text.NRSV: “A certain creditor had two debtors; one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.
text.Rotherham: Two debtors, there were, to a certain creditor,—the one, owed five hundred denaries, and, the other, fifty.

text.NRSV: When they could not pay, he canceled the debts for both of them. Now which of them will love him more?”
text.Rotherham: they not having wherewith to pay, he forgave, both. Which of them, therefore, will love him, more?

text.NRSV: Simon answered, “I suppose the one for whom he canceled the greater debt.” And Jesus said to him, “You have judged rightly.”
text.Rotherham: Making answer, Simon said—I suppose, that he to whom, the more, he forgave. And, he, said unto him—Rightly, hast thou judged.

text.NRSV: Then turning toward the woman, he said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered your house; you gave me no water for my feet, but she has bathed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.
text.Rotherham: And, turning towards the woman, unto Simon, he said—Seest thou this woman? I entered into thy house: water to me, on my feet, thou didst not give,—but, she, with her tears, hath wetted my feet, and, with her hair, wiped off [the tears] .

text.NRSV: You gave me no kiss, but from the time I came in she has not stopped kissing my feet.
text.Rotherham: A kiss, to me, thou didst not give,—but, she, from the time I came in, hath not ceased tenderly kissing my feet.

text.NRSV: You did not anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.
text.Rotherham: With oil, my head, thou didst not anoint,—but, she, with perfume, hath anointed, my feet.

text.NRSV: Therefore, I tell you, her sins, which were many, have been forgiven; hence she has shown great love. But the one to whom little is forgiven, loves little.”
text.Rotherham: For which cause, I say unto thee—Her many sins, have been forgiven, because she hath loved, much: but, he to whom little is forgiven, little, loveth.

text.NRSV: Then he said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto her—Thy sins have been forgiven.

text.NRSV: But those who were at the table with him began to say among themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”
text.Rotherham: And they who were reclining together, began to be saying within themselves—Who is, this, that, even forgiveth sins?

text.NRSV: And he said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you; go in peace.”
text.Rotherham: But he said unto the woman—Thy faith, hath saved thee,—Go thy way into peace.

text.NRSV: Soon afterwards he went on through cities and villages, proclaiming and bringing the good news of the kingdom of God. The twelve were with him,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in due course, that he was passing through, city by city and village by village, proclaiming, and delivering the glad-message of the kingdom of God,—and, the twelve, were with him;

text.NRSV: as well as some women who had been cured of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary, called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out,
text.Rotherham: and certain women, who had been cured from evil spirits and infirmities,—Mary, the one called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone forth,

text.NRSV: and Joanna, the wife of Herod’s steward Chuza, and Susanna, and many others, who provided for them out of their resources.
text.Rotherham: and Joana, wife of Chuza steward of Herod, and Susanna, and many others,—who indeed were ministering unto them out of their possessions.

text.NRSV: When a great crowd gathered and people from town after town came to him, he said in a parable:
text.Rotherham: And, seeing that a great multitude were coming together, and they who from every city were journeying forth unto him, he spake through means of a parable:—

text.NRSV: “A sower went out to sow his seed; and as he sowed, some fell on the path and was trampled on, and the birds of the air ate it up.
text.Rotherham: The sower went forth to sow his seed,—and, as he sowed, some, indeed, fell beside the pathway, and was trodden down; and, the birds of heaven, devoured it.

text.NRSV: Some fell on the rock; and as it grew up, it withered for lack of moisture.
text.Rotherham: And, other, fell down upon the rock; and, growing, was withered, because it had not moisture.

text.NRSV: Some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew with it and choked it.
text.Rotherham: And, other, fell and thorns; and, growing together, the thorns, choked it.

text.NRSV: Some fell into good soil, and when it grew, it produced a hundredfold.” As he said this, he called out, “Let anyone with ears to hear listen!”
text.Rotherham: And, other, fell into good ground; and, growing, brought forth fruit, a hundredfold. These things, saying, he went on to cry aloud—He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

text.NRSV: Then his disciples asked him what this parable meant.
text.Rotherham: But his disciples began to question him—What might, this very, parable be?

text.NRSV: He said, “To you it has been given to know the secrets of the kingdom of God; but to others I speak in parables, so that ‘looking they may not perceive, and listening they may not understand.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—Unto you, hath it been given, to get to know the sacred secrets of the kingdom of God; but, unto the rest, in parables,—in order that, seeing, they may not see, and, hearing, they may not understand.

text.NRSV: “Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.
text.Rotherham: Now the parable, is this—The seed, is, the word of God.

text.NRSV: The ones on the path are those who have heard; then the devil comes and takes away the word from their hearts, so that they may not believe and be saved.
text.Rotherham: And, those beside the pathway, are they who have heard; afterwards, cometh the adversary, and catcheth away the word from their heart, lest, believing, they should, be saved.

text.NRSV: The ones on the rock are those who, when they hear the word, receive it with joy. But these have no root; they believe only for a while and in a time of testing fall away.
text.Rotherham: And, those upon the rock, are they who, as soon as they hear, with joy, welcome the word; and, these, not having, root, are they who, for a season, believe,—and, in a season of testing, draw back.

text.NRSV: As for what fell among the thorns, these are the ones who hear; but as they go on their way they are choked by the cares and riches and pleasures of life, and their fruit does not mature.
text.Rotherham: And, that which in among the thorns fell, these, are they who have heard; and, by anxieties and wealth and pleasures of life being borne along, are choked up, and bear not to perfection.

text.NRSV: But as for that in the good soil, these are the ones who, when they hear the word, hold it fast in an honest and good heart, and bear fruit with patient endurance.
text.Rotherham: But, that in the good ground, these, are they who, indeed, in a noble and good heart having heard the word, hold fast, and bear fruit with endurance.

text.NRSV: “No one after lighting a lamp covers it with a vessel, or puts it under a bed, but puts it on a lampstand, so that those who enter may see the light.
text.Rotherham: And, no one having lit a lamp, covereth it up with a vessel, or, beneath a couch, putteth it; but, upon a lamp-stand, putteth it, that, they who come in, may see the light.

text.NRSV: For nothing is hidden that will not be disclosed, nor is anything secret that will not become known and come to light.
text.Rotherham: For there is no secret, which shall not be made, manifest; neither a hidden thing, which shall in anywise not be made known, or not come, where it can be seen.

text.NRSV: Then pay attention to how you listen; for to those who have, more will be given; and from those who have nothing, even what they seem to have will be taken away.”
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed therefore how ye hear; for, whosoever shall have, it shall be given unto him; and, whosoever shall not have, even what he seemeth to have, shall be taken from him.

text.NRSV: Then his mother and his brothers came to him, but they could not reach him because of the crowd.
text.Rotherham: And his mother and brethren came near unto him, and were unable to reach him, because of the multitude.

text.NRSV: And he was told, “Your mother and your brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.”
text.Rotherham: And it was reported to him—Thy mother and thy brethren, are standing outside, desiring, to see, thee.

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “My mother and my brothers are those who hear the word of God and do it.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering, said unto them—My mother and my brethren, are, these—they who, the word of God, are hearing and doing.

text.NRSV: One day he got into a boat with his disciples, and he said to them, “Let us go across to the other side of the lake.” So they put out,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on one of the days, that, he, entered into a boat, and his disciples; and he said unto them—Let us pass over unto the other side of the lake. And they set sail.

text.NRSV: and while they were sailing he fell asleep. A gale swept down on the lake, and the boat was filling with water, and they were in danger.
text.Rotherham: Now, as they were sailing, he fell asleep. And there came down a hurricane of wind upon the lake, and they began to be filled, and to be in peril.

text.NRSV: They went to him and woke him up, shouting, “Master, Master, we are perishing!” And he woke up and rebuked the wind and the raging waves; they ceased, and there was a calm.
text.Rotherham: And, coming near, they roused him up, saying—Master! Master! we perish! And, he, roused up, rebuked the wind, and the surging of the water; and they ceased, and it became a calm.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Where is your faith?” They were afraid and amazed, and said to one another, “Who then is this, that he commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him?”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Where was your faith? But, struck with fear, they marvelled, saying one to another—Who then is, this,—that, even unto the winds, he giveth orders, and unto the water, and they hearken unto him.

text.NRSV: Then they arrived at the country of the Gerasenes, which is opposite Galilee.
text.Rotherham: And they sailed down into the country of the Gerasenes, the which is over against Galilee.

text.NRSV: As he stepped out on land, a man of the city who had demons met him. For a long time he had worn no clothes, and he did not live in a house but in the tombs.
text.Rotherham: And, when he went forth upon the land, there met him a certain man, out of the city, having demons; and, for a considerable time, he had put on no garment, and, in a house, would not abide, but among the tombs.

text.NRSV: When he saw Jesus, he fell down before him and shouted at the top of his voice, “What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, do not torment me” —
text.Rotherham: But, seeing Jesus, he cried out aloud, and fell down before him, and, with a loud voice, said—What have I in common with thee, O Jesus, Son of [God] the Most High? I beg of thee—Do not torment me!—

text.NRSV: for Jesus had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. (For many times it had seized him; he was kept under guard and bound with chains and shackles, but he would break the bonds and be driven by the demon into the wilds.)
text.Rotherham: for he was about to charge the impure spirit to come out from the man. For, many times, had it snatched him away; and he used to be bound with chains and fetters, for a safe-guard, and was wont to break in pieces the bonds, and to be driven by the demon into the deserts.

text.NRSV: Jesus then asked him, “What is your name?” He said, “Legion”; for many demons had entered him.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus questioned him—What name hast thou? And, he, said—Legion! because many demons had entered into him.

text.NRSV: They begged him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
text.Rotherham: And they were beseeching him that he would not order them, into the abyss, to depart.

text.NRSV: Now there on the hillside a large herd of swine was feeding; and the demons begged Jesus to let them enter these. So he gave them permission.
text.Rotherham: Now there was there, a herd of a good many swine, feeding in the mountain; and they besought him, that he would suffer them, into those, to enter; and he suffered them.

text.NRSV: Then the demons came out of the man and entered the swine, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and was drowned.
text.Rotherham: And the demons, going forth from the man entered into the swine; and the herd rushed down the cliff, into the lake, and were choked.

text.NRSV: When the herdsmen saw what had happened, they ran off and told it in the city and in the country.
text.Rotherham: And they who had been feeding them, seeing what had happened, fled, and carried tidings into the city, and into the country-places.

text.NRSV: Then people came out to see what had happened, and when they came to Jesus, they found the man from whom the demons had gone sitting at the feet of Jesus, clothed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.
text.Rotherham: And they went forth to see what had happened; and came unto Jesus, and found the man from whom the demons had gone forth, sitting, clothed, and of sound mind, near the feet of Jesus,—and they were struck with fear.

text.NRSV: Those who had seen it told them how the one who had been possessed by demons had been healed.
text.Rotherham: And they who had seen it, reported unto them, how the demonized man was saved.

text.NRSV: Then all the people of the surrounding country of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave them; for they were seized with great fear. So he got into the boat and returned.
text.Rotherham: And one and all of the throng of the surrounding country of the Gerasenes, requested him, to depart from them; because, with a great fear, were they oppressed. And, he, entering into a boat, returned.

text.NRSV: The man from whom the demons had gone begged that he might be with him; but Jesus sent him away, saying,
text.Rotherham: And the man from whom the demons had gone forth, began to beg of him, that he might be with him; but he dismissed him, saying—

text.NRSV: “Return to your home, and declare how much God has done for you.” So he went away, proclaiming throughout the city how much Jesus had done for him.
text.Rotherham: Be returning unto thy house, and telling forth what great things, God, for thee, hath done. And he departed, through all the city, proclaiming what great things, Jesus, had done for him.

text.NRSV: Now when Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.
text.Rotherham: Now, when Jesus returned, the multitude welcomed him back, for they were all expecting him.

text.NRSV: Just then there came a man named Jairus, a leader of the synagogue. He fell at Jesus’ feet and begged him to come to his house,
text.Rotherham: And lo! there came a man, whose name was Jairus, and, the same, was, a ruler of the synagogue,—and, falling down near the feet of Jesus, he began beseeching him to enter into his house;

text.NRSV: for he had an only daughter, about twelve years old, who was dying. As he went, the crowds pressed in on him.
text.Rotherham: because he had, an only-begotten daughter, about twelve years old, and, she, was dying. Now, as he withdrew, the multitudes, were hemming him in.

text.NRSV: Now there was a woman who had been suffering from hemorrhages for twelve years, and though she had spent all she had on physicians, no one could cure her.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman, with a flow of blood of twelve years standing, who indeed could, from no one, be cured,

text.NRSV: She came up behind him and touched the fringe of his clothes, and immediately her hemorrhage stopped.
text.Rotherham: coming near behind, touched the fringe of his mantle; and, instantly, was stayed the flow of her blood.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus asked, “Who touched me?” When all denied it, Peter said, “Master, the crowds surround you and press in on you.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said—Who, is it that touched me? And, when all were denying, Peter said—Master! the multitudes, are hemming thee in, and pressing along.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said, “Someone touched me; for I noticed that power had gone out from me.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said—Some one touched me, for, I, took note of power, gone out from me.

text.NRSV: When the woman saw that she could not remain hidden, she came trembling, and falling down before him, she declared in the presence of all the people why she had touched him, and how she had been immediately healed.
text.Rotherham: And, the woman seeing that she had not escaped notice, trembling, came, and, falling down before him, reported before all the people, for what cause she had touched him, and how she was healed instantly!

text.NRSV: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said to her—Daughter! thy faith, hath saved thee: Go thy way into peace.

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking, someone came from the leader’s house to say, “Your daughter is dead; do not trouble the teacher any longer.”
text.Rotherham: While yet he is speaking, there cometh one from the synagogue ruler’s, saying—Thy daughter, is dead: No further, be troubling the teacher.

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard this, he replied, “Do not be afraid. Only believe, and she will be saved.”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, hearing, answered him—Do not fear! Only believe, and she shall be saved.

text.NRSV: When he came to the house, he did not allow anyone to enter with him, except Peter, John, and James, and the child’s father and mother.
text.Rotherham: And, when he came into the house, he suffered no one to enter with him, save Peter and John and James, and the father of the girl, and the mother.

text.NRSV: Everyone was weeping and wailing for her; but he said, “Do not weep; for she is not dead but sleeping.”
text.Rotherham: And they were all weeping, and beating themselves, for her. And he said—Be not weeping; for she died not, but is sleeping.

text.NRSV: And they laughed at him, knowing that she was dead.
text.Rotherham: And they were deriding him, knowing that she died.

text.NRSV: But he took her by the hand and called out, “Child, get up!”
text.Rotherham: But, he, grasping her hand, called aloud, saying—O girl! arise!

text.NRSV: Her spirit returned, and she got up at once. Then he directed them to give her something to eat.
