text.NRSV: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming near to listen to him.
text.Rotherham: But all the tax-collectors and the sinners were, unto him, drawing near, to be hearkening unto him;

text.NRSV: And the Pharisees and the scribes were grumbling and saying, “This fellow welcomes sinners and eats with them.”
text.Rotherham: and both the Pharisees and the Scribes were murmuring, saying, This man, unto sinners, giveth welcome, and eateth with them.

text.NRSV: So he told them this parable:
text.Rotherham: And he spake unto them this parable, saying—

text.NRSV: “Which one of you, having a hundred sheep and losing one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go after the one that is lost until he finds it?
text.Rotherham: What man from among you, having a hundred sheep, and losing, from among them, one, doth not leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go his way after the lost one, until he find it?

text.NRSV: When he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders and rejoices.
text.Rotherham: And, finding it, he layeth it upon his shoulders, rejoicing;

text.NRSV: And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep that was lost.’
text.Rotherham: and, coming unto his house, calleth together the friends and the neighbours, saying unto them—Rejoice with me! because I have found my sheep that was lost!

text.NRSV: Just so, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who need no repentance.
text.Rotherham: I say unto you—Thus, joy in heaven, will there be, over one sinner repenting, rather than over ninety-nine righteous persons, who indeed have, no need, of repentance.

text.NRSV: “Or what woman having ten silver coins, if she loses one of them, does not light a lamp, sweep the house, and search carefully until she finds it?
text.Rotherham: Or, what woman, having, ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a lamp, and sweep the house, and seek carefully, until she find it?

text.NRSV: When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin that I had lost.’
text.Rotherham: And, having found it, she calleth together her female friends and neighbours, saying—Rejoice with me! because I have found the piece of silver which I had lost.

text.NRSV: Just so, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, I say unto you, there ariseth joy in presence of the messengers of God, over one sinner repenting.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said, “There was a man who had two sons.
text.Rotherham: And he said—A certain man, had two sons.

text.NRSV: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the share of the property that will belong to me.’ So he divided his property between them.
text.Rotherham: And the younger of them said unto the father—Father! give me the share that falleth to me, of what there is. And, he, divided unto them the living.

text.NRSV: A few days later the younger son gathered all he had and traveled to a distant country, and there he squandered his property in dissolute living.
text.Rotherham: And, after not many days, the younger son, gathering all together, left home for a country far away, and, there, squandered his substance with riotous living.

text.NRSV: When he had spent everything, a severe famine help up throughout that country, and he began to be in need.
text.Rotherham: And, when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine throughout that country,—and, he, began to be in want.

text.NRSV: So he went and hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him to his fields to feed the pigs.
text.Rotherham: And he went his way, and joined himself unto one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to be feeding swine.

text.NRSV: He would gladly have filled himself with the pods that the pigs were eating; and no one gave him anything.
text.Rotherham: And he used to long to be filled with the pods which the swine were eating; and, no man, was giving unto him.

text.NRSV: But when he came to himself he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired hands have bread enough and to spare, but here I am dying of hunger!
text.Rotherham: But coming, to himself, he said—How many hired servants of my father, have bread enough and to spare, whereas, I, with famine, here, am perishing!

text.NRSV: I will get up and go to my father, and I will say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you;
text.Rotherham: I will arise, and go unto my father, and will say unto him—Father! I have sinned against heaven, and before thee:

text.NRSV: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; treat me like one of your hired hands.”’
text.Rotherham: No longer, am I worthy to be called a son of thine,—Make me as one of thy hired servants. And he arose, and came unto his own father.

text.NRSV: So he set off and went to his father. But while he was still far off, his father saw him and was filled with compassion; he ran and put his arms around him and kissed him.
text.Rotherham: Now, while yet he was holding afar off, his father saw him, and was moved with compassion, and, running, fell upon his neck, and tenderly kissed him.

text.NRSV: Then the son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
text.Rotherham: And the son said unto him—Father! I have sinned against heaven, and before thee: No longer, am I worthy to be called a son of thine,—[make me as one of thy hired servants.]

text.NRSV: But the father said to his slaves, ‘Quickly, bring out a robe—the best one—and put it on him; put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.
text.Rotherham: But the father said unto his servants—Quick! bring forth a robe—the best! and put on him, and get out a ring for his hand, and sandals for his feet.

text.NRSV: And get the fatted calf and kill it, and let us eat and celebrate;
text.Rotherham: And be bringing the fatted calf, sacrifice! and let us eat and make merry:

text.NRSV: for this son of mine was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found!’ And they began to celebrate.
text.Rotherham: Because, this my son, was, dead, and hath come to life again, was lost, and is found. And they began to be making merry.

text.NRSV: “Now his elder son was in the field; and when he came and approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
text.Rotherham: But his elder son was in a field; and, as, in coming, he drew near unto the house, he heard music and dancing,—

text.NRSV: He called one of the slaves and asked what was going on.
text.Rotherham: and, calling near one of the youths, he inquired what these things, could be.

text.NRSV: He replied, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fatted calf, because he has got him back safe and sound.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto him,—Thy brother, hath come, and thy father hath sacrificed the fatted calf, because, safe and sound, hath he received him back.

text.NRSV: Then he became angry and refused to go in. His father came out and began to plead with him.
text.Rotherham: But he was provoked to anger, and would not go in. And, his father, coming out, began to entreat him.

text.NRSV: But he answered his father, ‘Listen! For all these years I have been working like a slave for you, and I have never disobeyed your command; yet you have never given me even a young goat so that I might celebrate with my friends.
text.Rotherham: But, he, answering said unto his father—Lo! so many years as these, do I serve thee, and, at no time, a commandment of thine, have I transgressed,—And, unto me, at no time, hast thou given a kid, that, with my friends, I might make merry;

text.NRSV: But when this son of yours came back, who has devoured your property with prostitutes, you killed the fatted calf for him!’
text.Rotherham: But, when, this thy son, who had devoured thy living with harlots, came, thou didst sacrifice, for him, the fatted calf;

text.NRSV: Then the father said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto him—Child! thou, always, art, with me, and, all that is mine, is, thine;

text.NRSV: But we had to celebrate and rejoice, because this brother of yours was dead and has come to life; he was lost and has been found.’”
text.Rotherham: But, to make merry and rejoice, there was need, because, this thy brother—was, dead, and hath come to life again, and was lost, and is found.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to the disciples, “There was a rich man who had a manager, and charges were brought to him that this man was squandering his property.
text.Rotherham: And he went on to say, unto his disciples also,—There was, a certain rich man, who had a steward, and, the same, was accused to him as squandering his goods.

text.NRSV: So he summoned him and said to him, ‘What is this that I hear about you? Give me an accounting of your management, because you cannot be my manager any longer.’
text.Rotherham: And, accosting him, he said unto him—What is this I hear of thee? Render the account of thy stewardship, for thou canst no longer be steward.

text.NRSV: Then the manager said to himself, ‘What will I do, now that my master is taking the position away from me? I am not strong enough to dig, and I am ashamed to beg.
text.Rotherham: And the steward said within himself—What shall I do, because my lord taketh away the stewardship from me? Dig, I cannot: to beg, I am ashamed.

text.NRSV: I have decided what to do so that, when I am dismissed as manager, people may welcome me into their homes.’
text.Rotherham: I know what I will do, that, when I am removed out of the stewardship, they may welcome me into their own houses.

text.NRSV: So, summoning his master’s debtors one by one, he asked the first, ‘How much do you owe my master?’
text.Rotherham: And, calling unto him each one of the debtors of his own lord, he was saying unto the first,—How much owest thou my lord?

text.NRSV: He answered, ‘A hundred jugs of olive oil.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, sit down quickly, and make it fifty.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—A hundred baths of oil. And, he, said unto him—Kindly take thine accounts, and, sitting down, make haste and write—Fifty!

text.NRSV: Then he asked another, ‘And how much do you owe?’ He replied, ‘A hundred containers of wheat.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill and make it eighty.’
text.Rotherham: After that, unto another, he said—And how much owest, thou? And, he, said—A hundred homers of wheat. He saith unto him—Kindly take thine accounts, and write—Eighty!

text.NRSV: And his master commended the dishonest manager because he had acted shrewdly; for the children of this age are more shrewd in dealing with their own generation than are the children of light.
text.Rotherham: And the lord praised the unrighteous steward, in that with forethought he acted:—Because, the sons of this age, have more forethought than the sons of light, respecting their own generation.

text.NRSV: And I tell you, make friends for yourselves by means of dishonest wealth so that when it is gone, they may welcome you into the eternal homes.
text.Rotherham: And, I, unto you, say—For yourselves, make ye friends, with the unjust Riches, in order that, as soon as it shall fail, they may welcome you into the age-abiding tents.

text.NRSV: “Whoever is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much; and whoever is dishonest in a very little is dishonest also in much.
text.Rotherham: The faithful in least, in much also, is, faithful, and, he that in least is unrighteous, in much also, is, unrighteous.

text.NRSV: If then you have not been faithful with the dishonest wealth, who will entrust to you the true riches?
text.Rotherham: If therefore, in the unjust Riches ye proved unfaithful, the true, who, unto you, will entrust?

text.NRSV: And if you have not been faithful with what belongs to another, who will give you what is your own?
text.Rotherham: And, if, in what was another’s, ye proved unfaithful, your own, who will give unto you?

text.NRSV: No slave can serve two masters; for a slave will either hate the one and love the other, or be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.”
text.Rotherham: No domestic, can, unto two masters, be in service; for either, the one, he will hate, and, the other, love, or, unto the one, he will hold, and, the other, despise: Ye cannot, unto God, be in service, and unto Riches.

text.NRSV: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, heard all this, and they ridiculed him.
text.Rotherham: Now the Pharisees, who were, lovers of money, were hearing all these things, and were openly sneering at him.

text.NRSV: So he said to them, “You are those who justify yourselves in the sight of others; but God knows your hearts; for what is prized by human beings is an abomination in the sight of God.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Ye, are they who justify themselves before men, but, God, knoweth your hearts; because, that which amongst men is lofty, is an abomination before God.

text.NRSV: “The law and the prophets were until John came; since then the good news of the kingdom of God is proclaimed, and everyone tries to enter it by force.
text.Rotherham: The law and the prophets, were until John,—from that time, the good news of the kingdom of God, is being proclaimed, and, everyone, thereinto, is forcing his way.

text.NRSV: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away, than for one stroke of a letter in the law to be struck out.
text.Rotherham: But it is, easier, for heaven and earth to pass away, than that, of the law, one little point, should fail.

text.NRSV: “Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and whoever marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
text.Rotherham: Every one divorcing his wife and marrying another, committeth adultery; and, he that marrieth a woman divorced from a husband, committeth adultery.

text.NRSV: “There was a rich man who was dressed in purple and fine linen and who feasted sumptuously every day.
text.Rotherham: Now, a certain man, was rich, and he used to clothe himself with purple and fine linen, making merry day by day, brilliantly.

text.NRSV: And at his gate lay a poor man named Lazarus, covered with sores,
text.Rotherham: And, a certain beggar, by name Lazarus, used to be cast near his gate, full of sores,

text.NRSV: who longed to satisfy his hunger with what fell from the rich man’s table; even the dogs would come and lick his sores.
text.Rotherham: and to long to be fed from the crumbs that fell from the table of the rich man: nay! even, the dogs, used to come and lick his sores.

text.NRSV: The poor man died and was carried away by the angels to be with Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that the beggar died, and was carried away by the messengers, into the bosom of Abraham. And, the rich man also, died, and was buried.

text.NRSV: In Hades, where he was being tormented, he looked up and saw Abraham far away with Lazarus by his side.
text.Rotherham: And, in hades, lifting up his eyes, being in torments, he seeth Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.

text.NRSV: He called out, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue; for I am in agony in these flames.’
text.Rotherham: And, he, calling out, said—Father Abraham! have mercy upon me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue,—because I am in anguish in this flame.

text.NRSV: But Abraham said, ‘Child, remember that during your lifetime you received your good things, and Lazarus in like manner evil things; but now he is comforted here, and you are in agony.
text.Rotherham: But Abraham said—Child! remember—That thou didst duly received thy good things in thy life, and, Lazarus, in like manner, the evil things; but, now, here, he is comforted, and, thou, art in anguish.

text.NRSV: Besides all this, between us and you a great chasm has been fixed, so that those who might want to pass from here to you cannot do so, and no one can cross from there to us.’
text.Rotherham: And, besides all these things, betwixt us and you, a great chasm, hath been fixed,—so that, they who might wish to cross over from hence unto you, should not be able, nor any, from thence unto us, be crossing over.

text.NRSV: He said, ‘Then, father, I beg you to send him to my father’s house—
text.Rotherham: But he said—I request thee then, father, that thou wouldst send him unto my father’s house,—

text.NRSV: for I have five brothers—that he may warn them, so that they will not also come into this place of torment.’
text.Rotherham: for I have five brethren;—that he may solemnly testify unto them, lest, they also, come into this place of torment.

text.NRSV: Abraham replied, ‘They have Moses and the prophets; they should listen to them.’
text.Rotherham: But Abraham saith—They have Moses and the Prophets: Let them hearken unto them.

text.NRSV: He said, ‘No, father Abraham; but if someone goes to them from the dead, they will repent.’
text.Rotherham: But, he, said—Nay! father Abraham, but, if one, from the dead, should go unto them, they would repent.

text.NRSV: He said to him, ‘If they do not listen to Moses and the prophets, neither will they be convinced even if someone rises from the dead.’”
text.Rotherham: But he said unto him—If, unto Moses and the Prophets, they do not hearken, neither, if one, from among the dead, should arise, would they be persuaded.

verses.text: Jesus said to his disciples, “Occasions for stumbling are bound to come, but woe to anyone by whom they come!
It would be better for you if a millstone were hung around your neck and you were thrown into the sea than for you to cause one of these little ones to stumble.
Be on your guard! If another disciple sins, you must rebuke the offender, and if there is repentance, you must forgive.
And if the same person sins against you seven times a day, and turns back to you seven times and says, ‘I repent,’ you must forgive.”
The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith!”
The Lord replied, “If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.
“Who among you would say to your slave who has just come in from plowing or tending sheep in the field, ‘Come here at once and take your place at the table’?
Would you not rather say to him, ‘Prepare supper for me, put on your apron and serve me while I eat and drink; later you may eat and drink’?
Do you thank the slave for doing what was commanded?
So you also, when you have done all that you were ordered to do, say, ‘We are worthless slaves; we have done only what we ought to have done!’”
On the way to Jerusalem Jesus was going through the region between Samaria and Galilee.
As he entered a village, ten lepers approached him. Keeping their distance,
they called out, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”
When he saw them, he said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” And as they went, they were cleansed.
Then one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, praising God with a loud voice.
He prostrated himself at Jesus’ feet and thanked him. And he was a Samaritan.
Then Jesus asked, “Were not ten made clean? But the other nine, where are they?
Was none of them found to return and give praise to God except this foreigner?”
Then he said to him, “Get up and go on your way; your faith has made you well.”
Once Jesus was asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God was coming, and he answered, “The kingdom of God is not coming with things that can be observed;
nor will they say, ‘Look, here it is!’ or ‘There it is!’ For, in fact, the kingdom of God is among you.”
Then he said to the disciples, “The days are coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
They will say to you, ‘Look there!’ or ‘Look here!’ Do not go, do not set off in pursuit.
For as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one side to the other, so will the Son of Man be in his day.
But first he must endure much suffering and be rejected by this generation.
Just as it was in the days of Noah, so too it will be in the days of the Son of Man.
They were eating and drinking, and marrying and being given in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and destroyed all of them.
Likewise, just as it was in the days of Lot: they were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building,
but on the day that Lot left Sodom, it rained fire and sulfur from heaven and destroyed all of them.
—it will be like that on the day that the Son of Man is revealed.
On that day, anyone on the housetop who has belongings in the house must not come down to take them away; and likewise anyone in the field must not turn back.
Remember Lot’s wife.
Those who try to make their life secure will lose it, but those who lose their life will keep it.
I tell you, on that night there will be two in one bed; one will be taken and the other left.
There will be two women grinding meal together; one will be taken and the other left.”
Then they asked him, “Where, Lord?” He said to them, “Where the corpse is, there the vultures will gather.”

text.NRSV: Then Jesus told them a parable about their need to pray always and not to lose heart.
text.Rotherham: And he was speaking a parable unto them, as to its being needful for them always to pray, and not be faint-hearted;

text.NRSV: He said, “In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor had respect for people.
text.Rotherham: saying—A certain judge, there was in a certain city,—having, for God, no reverence, and, for man, no respect.

text.NRSV: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him and saying, ‘Grant me justice against my opponent.’
text.Rotherham: And, a widow, there was, in that city; and she kept coming unto him, saying—Vindicate me from mine adversary!

text.NRSV: For a while he refused; but later he said to himself, ‘Though I have no fear of God and no respect for anyone,
text.Rotherham: And he was unwilling for a time; but, after these things, he said within himself—Although, neither God, I reverence, nor man, I respect,

text.NRSV: yet because this widow keeps bothering me, I will grant her justice, so that she may not finally wear me out by her continual coming.’”
text.Rotherham: Yet, if only because this widow annoyeth me, I will vindicate her,—lest, persistently coming, she wholly wear me out.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, “Listen to what the unjust judge says.
text.Rotherham: And the Lord said—Hear ye what, the unrighteous judge saith;—

text.NRSV: And will not God grant justice to his chosen ones who cry to him day and night? Will he delay long in helping them?
text.Rotherham: And shall, God, in any wise not execute the vindication of his chosen ones, who are crying out to him day and night, although he beareth long with regard to them?

text.NRSV: I tell you, he will quickly grant justice to them. And yet, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth?”
text.Rotherham: I tell you—He will execute their vindication, quickly! nevertheless, though, the Son of Man, do come, will he, after all, find the faith on the earth?

text.NRSV: He also told this parable to some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous and regarded others with contempt:
text.Rotherham: And he spake, even unto certain who were confident in themselves that they were righteous, and were despising the rest, this parable:—

text.NRSV: “Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
text.Rotherham: Two men, went up into the temple to pray, one, a Pharisee, and, the other, a tax-collector.

text.NRSV: The Pharisee, standing by himself, was praying thus, ‘God, I thank you that I am not like other people: thieves, rogues, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.
text.Rotherham: The Pharisee, taking his stand, these things unto himself was praying: O God! I thank thee, that I am not like the rest of men,—extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or, even as this tax-collector;

text.NRSV: I fast twice a week; I give a tenth of all my income.’
text.Rotherham: I fast twice in the week, I give a tenth of whatsoever things I gain!

text.NRSV: But the tax collector, standing far off, would not even look up to heaven, but was beating his breast and saying, ‘God, be merciful to me, a sinner!’
text.Rotherham: But, the tax-collector, afar off, standing,—would not so much as lift up, his eyes, unto heaven, but kept smiting his own breast saying—O God! be propitiated unto me, the sinner!

text.NRSV: I tell you, this man went down to his home justified rather than the other; for all who exalt themselves will be humbled, but all who humble themselves will be exalted.”
text.Rotherham: I tell you—This one went down justified, unto his house, rather than that one; because, every one who exalteth himself, shall be abased, but, he that abaseth himself, shall be exalted?

text.NRSV: People were bringing even infants to him that he might touch them; and when the disciples saw it, they sternly ordered them not to do it.
text.Rotherham: And they were bringing unto him, even the babes, that he might touch, them; but the disciples, seeing it, began to rebuke them.

text.NRSV: But Jesus called for them and said, “Let the little children come to me, and do not stop them; for it is to such as these that the kingdom of God belongs.
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, called them near, saying—Suffer, the children, to be coming unto me, and do not hinder them; for, of such, is the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a little child will never enter it.”
text.Rotherham: Verily I say unto you—Whosoever shall not welcome the kingdom of God, as a child, in nowise shall enter thereinto.

text.NRSV: A certain ruler asked him, “Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
text.Rotherham: And a certain, ruler, questioned him, saying—Good Teacher! by doing what, shall I inherit life age-abiding?

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good but God alone.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus said to him—Why, callest thou me, good? None, is good, save one—God.

text.NRSV: You know the commandments: ‘You shall not commit adultery; You shall not murder; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness; Honor your father and mother.’”
text.Rotherham: The commandments, thou knowest:—Do not commit adultery, Do not commit murder, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness,—Honour thy father and mother,

text.NRSV: He replied, “I have kept all these since my youth.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—All these things, have I kept from my youth.

text.NRSV: When Jesus heard this, he said to him, “There is still one thing lacking. Sell all that you own and distribute the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, hearing, said unto him—Yet one thing, unto thee, is lacking: Whatsoever thou hast, sell, and distribute unto the destitute,—and thou shalt have treasure in [the] heavens; and come! be following me.

text.NRSV: But when he heard this, he became sad; for he was very rich.
text.Rotherham: But, he, hearing these things, became, encompassed with grief; for he was rich exceedingly.

text.NRSV: Jesus looked at him and said, “How hard it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, beholding him, said—With what difficulty, shall, they who have money, enter, into the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Indeed, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: For it is, easier, for a camel, to enter, through the eye of a needle, than for, a rich man, to enter, into the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Those who heard it said, “Then who can be saved?”
text.Rotherham: And they who heard said—Who, then, can be saved?

text.NRSV: He replied, “What is impossible for mortals is possible for God.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—The things impossible with men, are, possible with God.

text.NRSV: Then Peter said, “Look, we have left our homes and followed you.”
text.Rotherham: And Peter said—Lo! we, have left our own possessions, and followed thee!

text.NRSV: And he said to them, “Truly I tell you, there is no one who has left house or wife or brothers or parents or children, for the sake of the kingdom of God,
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—Verily, I say unto you—No one, is there, who hath left—house, or wife, or brethren, or parents, or children,—for the sake of the kingdom of God;

text.NRSV: who will not get back very much more in this age, and in the age to come eternal life.”
text.Rotherham: who shall in anywise not receive manifold in this season, and, in the age that is coming, life age-abiding.

text.NRSV: Then he took the twelve aside and said to them, “See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that is written about the Son of Man by the prophets will be accomplished.
text.Rotherham: And, taking aside the twelve, he said unto them—Lo! we are going up unto Jerusalem, and all the things will be finished, which have been written through means of the prophets, respecting the Son of Man;

text.NRSV: For he will be handed over to the Gentiles; and he will be mocked and insulted and spat upon.
text.Rotherham: For he will be delivered up unto the nations, and be mocked, and insulted, and spit upon,—

text.NRSV: After they have flogged him, they will kill him, and on the third day he will rise again.”
text.Rotherham: and, having scourged him, they will slay him, and, on the third day, will, he, arise.

text.NRSV: But they understood nothing about all these things; in fact, what he said was hidden from them, and they did not grasp what was said.
text.Rotherham: And, they, not one of these things, understood, and this saying was hidden from them, and the could not comprehend the things spoken.

text.NRSV: As he approached Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the roadside begging.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he was drawing near unto Jericho, a certain blind man, was sitting beside the road, begging.

text.NRSV: When he heard a crowd going by, he asked what was happening.
text.Rotherham: And. hearkening unto a multitude moving along, he enquired, what this, might be.

text.NRSV: They told him, “Jesus of Nazareth is passing by.”
text.Rotherham: And they told him—Jesus of Nazareth, is passing by!

text.NRSV: Then he shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
text.Rotherham: And he cried aloud, saying—Jesus, son of David! have mercy upon me!

text.NRSV: Those who were in front sternly ordered him to be quiet; but he shouted even more loudly, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
text.Rotherham: And, they that were going before, began to rebuke him, that he might hold his peace. But, he, by so much the more, was crying out—O Son of David! have mercy upon me!

text.NRSV: Jesus stood still and ordered the man to be brought to him; and when he came near, he asked him,
text.Rotherham: And, standing still, Jesus commanded him to be led unto him; and, when he had drawn near, he questioned him—

text.NRSV: “What do you want me to do for you?” He said, “Lord, let me see again.”
text.Rotherham: What desirest thou, I should do unto thee? And, he, said—Lord! …that I may recover sight!

text.NRSV: Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight; your faith has saved you.”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto him—Recover sight! Thy faith, hath saved thee.

text.NRSV: Immediately he regained his sight and followed him, glorifying God; and all the people, when they saw it, praised God.
text.Rotherham: And, instantly, he recovered sight, and began to follow him, glorifying God. And, all the people, beholding, gave praise unto God.

text.NRSV: He entered Jericho and was passing through it.
text.Rotherham: And, having entered, he was passing through Jericho;

text.NRSV: A man was there named Zacchaeus; he was a chief tax collector and was rich.
text.Rotherham: and lo! a man, by name called Zacchaeus, and, he, was a chief tax-collector, and [he] was rich.

text.NRSV: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but on account of the crowd he could not, because he was short in stature.
text.Rotherham: And he was seeking to see Jesus, what sort of man he was, and could not for the multitude, because, in stature, he was, small.

text.NRSV: So he ran ahead and climbed a sycamore tree to see him, because he was going to pass that way.
text.Rotherham: And, running forward unto the front, he got up a sycamore-tree, that he might see him; for, by that way, was he about to pass.

text.NRSV: When Jesus came to the place, he looked up and said to him, “Zacchaeus, hurry and come down; for I must stay at your house today.”
text.Rotherham: And, as he came up to the place, looking up, Jesus said unto him—Zacchaeus! make haste, and come down; For, to-day, in thy house, I must needs abide.

text.NRSV: So he hurried down and was happy to welcome him.
text.Rotherham: And he made haste, and came down, and received him joyfully.

text.NRSV: All who saw it began to grumble and said, “He has gone to be the guest of one who is a sinner.”
text.Rotherham: And all, when they beheld, began to murmur, saying—With a sinful man, hath he gone in to lodge!

text.NRSV: Zacchaeus stood there and said to the Lord, “Look, half of my possessions, Lord, I will give to the poor; and if I have defrauded anyone of anything, I will pay back four times as much.”
text.Rotherham: But, taking his stand, Zacchaeus said unto the Lord—Lo! the half of my possessions, Lord, unto the destitute, I give; and, if, from anyone, I have taken aught by false accusation, I give back fourfold.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to him, “Today salvation has come to this house, because he too is a son of Abraham.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto him—This day, salvation, unto this house, hath come,—for that, he too, is, a son of Abraham;

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man came to seek out and to save the lost.”
text.Rotherham: For the Son of Man came, to seek and to save what was lost.

text.NRSV: As they were listening to this, he went on to tell a parable, because he was near Jerusalem, and because they supposed that the kingdom of God was to appear immediately.
text.Rotherham: And, because they were hearing these things, he added and spake a parable, because of his being near Jerusalem, and their supposing that, instantly, was the kingdom of God to shine forth.

text.NRSV: So he said, “A nobleman went to a distant country to get royal power for himself and then return.
text.Rotherham: He said, therefore—A certain man, of noble birth, went into a country far away, to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.

text.NRSV: He summoned ten of his slaves, and gave them ten pounds, and said to them, ‘Do business with these until I come back.’
text.Rotherham: And, calling ten servants of his own, he gave unto them ten minas, and said unto them—Do business, till I come.

text.NRSV: But the citizens of his country hated him and sent a delegation after him, saying, ‘We do not want this man to rule over us.’
text.Rotherham: But, his citizens, hated him, and sent off an embassy after him, saying—We desire not, this, man, to be made king over us!

text.NRSV: When he returned, having received royal power, he ordered these slaves, to whom he had given the money, to be summoned so that he might find out what they had gained by trading.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when he returned, having received the kingdom, that he bade be called unto him these servants, to whom he had given the silver, that he might take note, what business they had done.

text.NRSV: The first came forward and said, ‘Lord, your pound has made ten more pounds.’
text.Rotherham: And the first came near, saying—Lord! thy mina, hath made, ten minas.

text.NRSV: He said to him, ‘Well done, good slave! Because you have been trustworthy in a very small thing, take charge of ten cities.’
text.Rotherham: And he said to him—Well done! good servant. Because, in a very small thing, thou hast been, faithful, have thou authority over, ten cities.

text.NRSV: Then the second came, saying, ‘Lord, your pound has made five pounds.’
text.Rotherham: And the second came, saying—Thy mina, lord, hath made five minas.

text.NRSV: He said to him, ‘And you, rule over five cities.’
text.Rotherham: And he said, to him also, And, thou, be over five cities.

text.NRSV: Then the other came, saying, ‘Lord, here is your pound. I wrapped it up in a piece of cloth,
text.Rotherham: And, the other, came, saying—Lord, lo! thy mina, which I kept lying by in a napkin;

text.NRSV: for I was afraid of you, because you are a harsh man; you take what you did not deposit, and reap what you did not sow.’
text.Rotherham: For I was afraid of thee, because, a harsh man, thou art,—Thou takest up, what thou layedst not down, and reapest, what thou sowedst not!

text.NRSV: He said to him, ‘I will judge you by your own words, you wicked slave! You knew, did you, that I am a harsh man, taking what I did not deposit and reaping what I did not sow?
text.Rotherham: He saith to him—Out of thy mouth, do I judge thee, O wicked servant! Thou knewest that, I, a harsh man, am,—taking up, what I laid not down, and reaping, what I did not sow;

text.NRSV: Why then did you not put my money into the bank? Then when I returned, I could have collected it with interest.’
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, then, didst thou not place my silver upon a [money-changer’s] table, and, I, when I came, with interest might have exacted it?

text.NRSV: He said to the bystanders, ‘Take the pound from him and give it to the one who has ten pounds.’
text.Rotherham: And, unto the by-standers, he said—Take, from him, the mina, and give unto him that hath, the ten minas;—

text.NRSV: (And they said to him, ‘Lord, he has ten pounds!’)
text.Rotherham: And they said to him, Lord! he hath ten minas;—

text.NRSV: ‘I tell you, to all those who have, more will be given; but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
text.Rotherham: I tell you—Unto everyone that hath, shall be given, whereas, from him that hath not, even what he hath, shall be taken away.

text.NRSV: But as for these enemies of mine who did not want me to be king over them—bring them here and slaughter them in my presence.’”
text.Rotherham: But, these mine enemies who desired not that I should be made king over them, bring ye here, and slay them outright before me.

text.NRSV: After he had said this, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, having said these things, he was moving on in front, going up into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: When he had come near Bethphage and Bethany, at the place called the Mount of Olives, he sent two of the disciples,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he drew near unto Bethphage, and Bethany, unto the mount which is called the Mount of Olives, he sent off two of his disciples,

text.NRSV: saying, “Go into the village ahead of you, and as you enter it you will find tied there a colt that has never been ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
text.Rotherham: saying—Go your way unto the opposite village, in which, as ye are entering, ye shall find a colt, bound, whereon, no man, hath, ever sat,—and, having loosed him, bring him.

text.NRSV: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you untying it?’ just say this, ‘The Lord needs it.’”
text.Rotherham: And, if any one question you—Wherefore are ye loosing him? thus, shall ye say—His Lord, hath, need.

text.NRSV: So those who were sent departed and found it as he had told them.
text.Rotherham: And they who were sent, departing, found as he had said to them.

text.NRSV: As they were untying the colt, its owners asked them, “Why are you untying the colt?”
text.Rotherham: And, as they were loosing the colt, his lords said unto them—Why are ye loosing the colt?

text.NRSV: They said, “The Lord needs it.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—His Lord, hath, need.

text.NRSV: Then they brought it to Jesus; and after throwing their cloaks on the colt, they set Jesus on it.
text.Rotherham: And they brought him unto Jesus; and, throwing their mantles over the colt, they seated Jesus thereon.

text.NRSV: As he rode along, people kept spreading their cloaks on the road.
text.Rotherham: And, as he moved along, they were spreading their own mantles in the way.

text.NRSV: As he was now approaching the path down from the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to praise God joyfully with a loud voice for all the deeds of power that they had seen,
text.Rotherham: And, when he was drawing near, already, unto the descent of the Mount of Olives, one-and-all the throng of the disciples began joyfully to be praising God with a loud voice, concerning all the mighty works which they had seen;

text.NRSV: saying, “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven, and glory in the highest heaven!”
text.Rotherham: saying—Blessed is he that cometh… The king! In the name of the Lord! In heaven, peace! And glory in the highest!

text.NRSV: Some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to him, “Teacher, order your disciples to stop.”
text.Rotherham: And, certain of the Pharisees from the multitude, said unto him—Teacher! rebuke thy disciples.

text.NRSV: He answered, “I tell you, if these were silent, the stones would shout out.”
text.Rotherham: And he answered and said—I tell you—If, these, shall hold their peace, the stones, will cry out.

text.NRSV: As he came near and saw the city, he wept over it,
text.Rotherham: And, when he drew near, beholding the city, he wept over it, saying—

text.NRSV: saying, “If you, even you, had only recognized on this day the things that make for peace! But now they are hidden from your eyes.
text.Rotherham: If thou hadst got to know, in this day, even thou, the conditions of peace… But, now, are they hid from thine eyes:

text.NRSV: Indeed, the days will come upon you, when your enemies will set up ramparts around you and surround you, and hem you in on every side.
text.Rotherham: Because days will have come upon thee, that thine enemies will throw around a rampart against thee, and enclose thee; and hem thee in from every side,—

text.NRSV: They will crush you to the ground, you and your children within you, and they will not leave within you one stone upon another; because you did not recognize the time of your visitation from God.”
text.Rotherham: And will level thee with the ground, and thy children within thee, and will not leave, stone on stone, within thee: because thou didst not get to know the season of thy visitation.

text.NRSV: Then he entered the temple and began to drive out those who were selling things there,
text.Rotherham: And, entering into the temple, he began to be casting out them who were selling;

text.NRSV: and he said, “It is written, ‘My house shall be a house of prayer’; but you have made it a den of robbers.”
text.Rotherham: saying unto them—It is written, And, my house, shall be, a house of prayer; but, ye, have made, it, a den of robbers.

text.NRSV: Every day he was teaching in the temple. The chief priests, the scribes, and the leaders of the people kept looking for a way to kill him;
text.Rotherham: And he was teaching day by day in the temple; but, the High-priests and the Scribes, also the chiefs of the people, were seeking to destroy him,—

text.NRSV: but they did not find anything they could do, for all the people were spellbound by what they heard.
text.Rotherham: and could not find what they might do, for, the people, one and all, were hanging upon him, as they hearkened.

text.NRSV: One day, as he was teaching the people in the temple and telling the good news, the chief priests and the scribes came with the elders
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on one of the days, as he was teaching the people in the temple, and telling the good tidings, that the High-priests and the Scribes, with the Elders, came upon him,—

text.NRSV: and said to him, “Tell us, by what authority are you doing these things? Who is it who gave you this authority?”
text.Rotherham: and spake, saying, unto him—Tell us, by what authority, these things, thou art doing, and, who, is it, that gave thee this authority.

text.NRSV: He answered them, “I will also ask you a question, and you tell me:
text.Rotherham: And answering, he said unto them—I too, will question, you, as to a matter,—and tell me!

text.NRSV: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin?”
text.Rotherham: The immersion of John, Of heaven, was it, or of men?

text.NRSV: They discussed it with one another, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why did you not believe him?’
text.Rotherham: And they deliberated together by themselves, saying—If we say, Of heaven, he will say, Wherefore did ye not believe him?

text.NRSV: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ all the people will stone us; for they are convinced that John was a prophet.”
text.Rotherham: But, if we say, Of men, the people, one and all, will stone us, for, persuaded, they are, that, John, was, a prophet.

text.NRSV: So they answered that they did not know where it came from.
text.Rotherham: And they answered, that they knew not whence.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”
text.Rotherham: And, Jesus, said unto them—Neither do, I, tell, you, by what authority, these things, I am doing.

text.NRSV: He began to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard, and leased it to tenants, and went to another country for a long time.
text.Rotherham: And he began, unto the people, to be speaking this parable:—A man, planted a vineyard, and let it out to husbandmen, and went from home for a long time.

text.NRSV: When the time came, he sent a slave to the tenants in order that they might give him his share of the produce of the vineyard; but the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: And, in due season, he sent unto the husbandmen, a servant, that, of the fruit of the vineyard, they might give him; but, the husbandmen, having beaten him, sent him away empty.

text.NRSV: Next he sent another slave; that one also they beat and insulted and sent away empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: And he, further, sent another servant; but, they, beating, that one also, and dishonouring him, sent him away, empty.

text.NRSV: He sent still a third; this one also they wounded and threw out.
text.Rotherham: And he, further, sent, a third; but, they, bruising, this one also, thrust him out.

text.NRSV: Then the owner of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect him.’
text.Rotherham: And the lord of the vineyard said—What shall I do? I will send my son, the beloved,—It may be, that, him, they will respect.

text.NRSV: But when the tenants saw him, they discussed it among themselves, saying, ‘This is the heir; let us kill him so that the inheritance may be ours.’
text.Rotherham: But he husbandmen, seeing him, began to deliberate one with another, saying—This, is, the heir: Let us slay him, that, ours, may be, the inheritance.

text.NRSV: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
text.Rotherham: And, thrusting him forth outside the vineyard, they slew him. What, then, will the lord of the vineyard do unto them?

text.NRSV: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.” When they heard this, they said, “Heaven forbid!”
text.Rotherham: He will come, and destroy these husbandmen, and give the vineyard unto others. But, when they heard it, they said—Far be it!

text.NRSV: But he looked at them and said, “What then does this text mean: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone’?
text.Rotherham: But, he, looking at them, said—What, then, is this that is written,—A stone which the builders’ rejected, The same, hath become, head of the corner?

text.NRSV: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces; and it will crush anyone on whom it falls.”
text.Rotherham: Every one who falleth on that stone, will be sorely bruised, but, on whomsoever it shall fall, it will utterly destroy him.

text.NRSV: When the scribes and chief priests realized that he had told this parable against them, they wanted to lay hands on him at that very hour, but they feared the people.
text.Rotherham: And the Scribes and the High-priests sought to thrust on him their hands, in that very hour; and feared the people; for they perceived that, against them, spake he this parable.

text.NRSV: So they watched him and sent spies who pretended to be honest, in order to trap him by what he said, so as to hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
text.Rotherham: And, watching narrowly, they sent forth suborned men, feigning themselves to be, righteous, that they might lay hold of a word of his, so as to deliver him up unto the rule and the authority of the governor.

text.NRSV: So they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you are right in what you say and teach, and you show deference to no one, but teach the way of God in accordance with truth.
text.Rotherham: And they questioned him, saying—Teacher! we know that, rightly, thou speakest and teachest, and respectest no person, but, in truth, the way of God, dost teach:

text.NRSV: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?”
text.Rotherham: Is it allowable for us to give, unto Caesar, tribute, or not?

text.NRSV: But he perceived their craftiness and said to them,
text.Rotherham: But, observing their villainy, he said unto them—

text.NRSV: “Show me a denarius. Whose head and whose title does it bear?” They said, “The emperor’s.”
text.Rotherham: Shew me a denary. Of whom, hath it an image and inscription? And, they, said—Of Caesar.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Then give to the emperor the things that are the emperor’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—Well then! render the things of Caesar, unto Caesar, and the things of God, unto God.

text.NRSV: And they were not able in the presence of the people to trap him by what he said; and being amazed by his answer, they became silent.
text.Rotherham: And they could not lay hold of the saying, before the people; and, marvelling at his answer, they held their peace.

text.NRSV: Some Sadducees, those who say there is no resurrection, came to him
text.Rotherham: But there came near certain of the Sadducees, they who say, Resurrection, there is none! and questioned him,

text.NRSV: and asked him a question, “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies, leaving a wife but no children, the man shall marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
text.Rotherham: saying—Teacher! Moses, wrote for us, If one’s brother die, having a wife, and, he, be, childless, that his brother shall take the wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.

text.NRSV: Now there were seven brothers; the first married a woman and died childless;
text.Rotherham: Seven brethren, therefore, there were,—and, the first, taking a wife, died childless,

text.NRSV: then the second
text.Rotherham: And, the second,

text.NRSV: and the third married her, and so in the same way all seven died childless.
text.Rotherham: and the third, took her,—yea, likewise, even the seven; They left no children, and died:

text.NRSV: Finally the woman also died.
text.Rotherham: Later on, the woman also, died.

text.NRSV: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife will the woman be? For the seven had married her.”
text.Rotherham: The woman, therefore, in the resurrection, Of which of them, doth she become wife? for, the seven, had her to wife.

text.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Those who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage;
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto them—The sons of this age, marry, and are given in marriage,—

text.NRSV: but those who are considered worthy of a place in that age and in the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.
text.Rotherham: But, they who have been accounted worthy, that age, to obtain, and the resurrection that is from among the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage;

text.NRSV: Indeed they cannot die anymore, because they are like angels and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.
text.Rotherham: For they cannot, even die any more,—for, equal unto messengers, are they, and are, sons of God, Of the resurrection, being, sons.

text.NRSV: And the fact that the dead are raised Moses himself showed, in the story about the bush, where he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: But, that the dead do rise, even Moses, disclosed at the bush, when he calleth the Lord—The God of Abraham and God of Isaac and God of Jacob:

text.NRSV: Now he is God not of the dead, but of the living; for to him all of them are alive.”
text.Rotherham: Now, God, he is not, of the dead, but, of the living,—for, all, unto him, do live.

text.NRSV: Then some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you have spoken well.”
text.Rotherham: And certain of the Scribes, answering, said—Teacher! Well, hast thou spoken.

text.NRSV: For they no longer dared to ask him another question.
text.Rotherham: For, no longer, were they daring to ask him any questions.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “How can they say that the Messiah is David’s son?
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—How say they, that, the Christ, is, David’s Son?

text.NRSV: For David himself says in the book of Psalms, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,
text.Rotherham: For, David himself, saith, in [the] book of Psalms: Said the Lord, unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand,

text.NRSV: until I make your enemies your footstool.”’
text.Rotherham: Until I make thy foes thy footstool.

text.NRSV: David thus calls him Lord; so how can he be his son?”
text.Rotherham: David, therefore, calleth, him Lord: How, then, his son, is he?

text.NRSV: In the hearing of all the people he said to the disciples,
text.Rotherham: And, all the people hearing, he said unto the disciples:

text.NRSV: “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and love to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces, and to have the best seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets.
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed of the Scribes—Who desire to walk about in long robes, and are fond of salutations in the markets, and first seats in the synagogues, and first couches in the chief meals,—

text.NRSV: They devour widows’ houses and for the sake of appearance say long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation.”
text.Rotherham: Who devour the houses of widows, and, for a show, are a long time at prayer. These, shall receive, a heavier sentence.

text.NRSV: He looked up and saw rich people putting their gifts into the treasury;
text.Rotherham: And, looking up, he saw the, rich, who were casting their gifts into the treasury,—

text.NRSV: he also saw a poor widow put in two small copper coins.
text.Rotherham: and he saw a certain poor widow, casting in thither two mites;

text.NRSV: He said, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put in more than all of them;
text.Rotherham: and he said—Of a truth, I say unto you—This destitute widow, more than they all, hath cast in;

text.NRSV: for all of them have contributed out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on.”
text.Rotherham: For, all these, out of their superfluity, have cast in among the gifts, but, she, out of her deficiency, all the living that she had, hath cast in.

text.NRSV: When some were speaking about the temple, how it was adorned with beautiful stones and gifts dedicated to God, he said,
text.Rotherham: And, certain saying, of the temple—With beautiful stones and offerings, hath it been adorned! he said—

text.NRSV: “As for these things that you see, the days will come when not one stone will be left upon another; all will be thrown down.”
text.Rotherham: As to these things, which ye are looking upon, There will come days, in which there will not be left here, stone upon stone, which will not be taken down.

text.NRSV: They asked him, “Teacher, when will this be, and what will be the sign that this is about to take place?”
text.Rotherham: And they questioned him, saying—Teacher! when, therefore, will these things be? And, what the sign, when these things shall be about to come to pass?

text.NRSV: And he said, “Beware that you are not led astray; for many will come in my name and say, ‘I am he!’ and, ‘The time is near!’ Do not go after them.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said—Be taking heed ye be not deceived; for, many, will come upon my name, saying—I, am he,—and—The season, hath drawn near! Do not go after them.

text.NRSV: “When you hear of wars and insurrections, do not be terrified; for these things must take place first, but the end will not follow immediately.”
text.Rotherham: But, whensoever ye shall hear of wars and revolutions, be not terrified,—for these things, must needs, come to pass first, but, not immediately, is the end.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom;
text.Rotherham: Then, said he unto them—There will rise up, nation against nation, and kingdom against kingdom;

text.NRSV: there will be great earthquakes, and in various places famines and plagues; and there will be dreadful portents and great signs from heaven.
text.Rotherham: As well great earthquakes, as also, in places, pestilences and famines, will there be, as well objects of terror, as also, from heaven, great signs, will there be.

text.NRSV: “But before all this occurs, they will arrest you and persecute you; they will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
text.Rotherham: And before, all these things, they will thrust upon you their hands, and persecute you, delivering you up into the synagogues and prisons,—when ye have been led away before kings and governors, for the sake of my name;

text.NRSV: This will give you an opportunity to testify.
text.Rotherham: But it shall turn out to you for a witness.

text.NRSV: So make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance;
text.Rotherham: Settle, then, in your hearts, not to be studying beforehand, how to make defense;

text.NRSV: for I will give you words and a wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to withstand or contradict.
text.Rotherham: For, I, will give you a mouth and wisdom, which one-and-all who are setting themselves against, you shall be unable to withstand or gainsay.

text.NRSV: You will be betrayed even by parents and brothers, by relatives and friends; and they will put some of you to death.
text.Rotherham: But ye will be delivered up, even by parents, and brethren, and kinsfolk, and friends, and they will put to death some from among you;

text.NRSV: You will be hated by all because of my name.
text.Rotherham: And ye will be hated by all, because of my name:

text.NRSV: But not a hair of your head will perish.
text.Rotherham: And, a hair of your head, in nowise shall perish,—

text.NRSV: By your endurance you will gain your souls.
text.Rotherham: By your endurance, shall ye gain your lives for a possession.

text.NRSV: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation has come near.
text.Rotherham: But whensoever ye shall see Jerusalem, encompassed by armies, then, know, that her desolation hath drawn near.

text.NRSV: Then those in Judea must flee to the mountains, and those inside the city must leave it, and those out in the country must not enter it;
text.Rotherham: Then they who are in Judaea, let them flee into the mountains, and they who are in her midst, let them go forth,—and they who are in the fields, let them not enter into her;

text.NRSV: for these are days of vengeance, as a fulfillment of all that is written.
text.Rotherham: For, days of avenging, are, these, for all the things written to be fulfilled.

text.NRSV: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days! For there will be great distress on the earth and wrath against this people;
text.Rotherham: Alas! for the women with child, and for them who are giving suck, in those days; for there will be great distress upon the land, and anger against this people.

text.NRSV: they will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations; and Jerusalem will be trampled on by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
text.Rotherham: And they will fall by the edge of the sword, and be carried away captive into all the nations, and, Jerusalem, shall be trodden down by the nations, until the seasons of the nations shall be fulfilled [and shall be].

text.NRSV: “There will be signs in the sun, the moon, and the stars, and on the earth distress among nations confused by the roaring of the sea and the waves.
text.Rotherham: And there will he signs in sun, and moon, and stars, and, on the earth, anguish of nations in embarrassment—sea and surge resounding,—

text.NRSV: People will faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
text.Rotherham: Men fainting, from fear and expectation of the things overtaking the inhabited earth. For, the powers of the heavens, will be shaken.

text.NRSV: Then they will see ‘the Son of Man coming in a cloud’ with power and great glory.
text.Rotherham: And, then, will they see the Son of Man—coming in a cloud, with great power and glory.

text.NRSV: Now when these things begin to take place, stand up and raise your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”
text.Rotherham: And, when these things are beginning to come to pass, unbend and lift up your heads, because that, your redemption, is drawing near.

text.NRSV: Then he told them a parable: “Look at the fig tree and all the trees;
text.Rotherham: And he spake a parable unto them: See the fig-tree, and all the trees,—

text.NRSV: as soon as they sprout leaves you can see for yourselves and know that summer is already near.
text.Rotherham: Whensoever they have already budded, seeing it, of yourselves, ye observe that, already near, is, the summer:

text.NRSV: So also, when you see these things taking place, you know that the kingdom of God is near.
text.Rotherham: Thus, ye also, whensoever ye shall see, these, things coming to pass, observe ye, that, near, is the kingdom of God!

text.NRSV: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things have taken place.
text.Rotherham: Verily, I say unto you—In nowise shall this generation pass away, until, all things, shall happen:

text.NRSV: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
text.Rotherham: Heaven and earth, will pass away, but, my word, in nowise will pass away.

text.NRSV: “Be on guard so that your hearts are not weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of this life, and that day catch you unexpectedly,
text.Rotherham: But be taking heed unto yourselves, lest once your hearts be made heavy—with debauch and drunkenness and anxieties about livelihood, and that day come upon you suddenly,

text.NRSV: like a trap. For it will come upon all who live on the face of the whole earth.
text.Rotherham: as a snare,—For it will come in by surprise, upon all them that are dwelling on the face of all the earth.

text.NRSV: Be alert at all times, praying that you may have the strength to escape all these things that will take place, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
text.Rotherham: But be watching in every season, making supplication, that ye may gain full vigour, to escape all these things that are about to be coming to pass, and to stand before the Son of Man.

text.NRSV: Every day he was teaching in the temple, and at night he would go out and spend the night on the Mount of Olives, as it was called.
text.Rotherham: And he was, by day, in the temple, teaching; but, by night, going forth, he was lodging in the mount which is called the Mount of Olives.

text.NRSV: And all the people would get up early in the morning to listen to him in the temple.
text.Rotherham: And, all the people, were coming at day-break unto him, in the temple, to be hearkening unto him.

text.NRSV: Now the festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Passover, was near.
text.Rotherham: And the feast of the unleavened bread, which is called a Passover, was drawing near.

text.NRSV: The chief priests and the scribes were looking for a way to put Jesus to death, for they were afraid of the people.
text.Rotherham: And the High-priests and the Scribes were seeking, how they might get rid of him; for they feared the people.

text.NRSV: Then Satan entered into Judas called Iscariot, who was one of the twelve;
text.Rotherham: But Satan entered into Judas, him called Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve;

text.NRSV: he went away and conferred with the chief priests and officers of the temple guard about how he might betray him to them.
text.Rotherham: and, departing, he conversed with the High-priests and Captains, as to how, unto them, he might deliver him up.

text.NRSV: They were greatly pleased and agreed to give him money.
text.Rotherham: And they rejoiced, and bargained with him to give him, silver;

text.NRSV: So he consented and began to look for an opportunity to betray him to them when no crowd was present.
text.Rotherham: and he freely consented, and began seeking a good opportunity for delivering him up, in the absence of a multitude, unto them.

text.NRSV: Then came the day of Unleavened Bread, on which the Passover lamb had to be sacrificed.
text.Rotherham: Now the day of the unleavened bread a came,—on which it was needful to be sacrificing the passover.

text.NRSV: So Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover meal for us that we may eat it.”
text.Rotherham: And he sent forth Peter and John, saying—Go, and make ready, for us, the passover, that we may eat.

text.NRSV: They asked him, “Where do you want us to make preparations?”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said unto him—Where wiliest thou, we should make ready?

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Listen: when you have entered the city, a man carrying a jar of water will meet you; follow him into the house he enters
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—Lo! when ye have entered into the city, there will meet you a man, bearing, an earthen-jar of water: follow him into the house into which he is entering.

text.NRSV: and say to the owner of the house, ‘The Teacher asks you, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’
text.Rotherham: And ye shall say unto the master of the house—The teacher saith unto thee, where is the lodging, where, the passover, with my disciples, I may eat?

text.NRSV: He will show you a large room upstairs, already furnished. Make preparations there.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, unto you, will shew, a large upper room, spread: there, make ready.

text.NRSV: So they went and found everything as he had told them; and they prepared the Passover meal.
text.Rotherham: And, departing, they found according as he had said unto them, and made ready the passover.

text.NRSV: When the hour came, he took his place at the table, and the apostles with him.
text.Rotherham: And, when the hour arrived, he reclined, and the apostles with him.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer;
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—I have greatly desired, to eat, this passover, with you before I suffer;

text.NRSV: for I tell you, I will not eat it until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.”
text.Rotherham: For, I say unto you—In nowise shall I eat it, until it have been fulfilled in the kingdom of God.

text.NRSV: Then he took a cup, and after giving thanks he said, “Take this and divide it among yourselves;
text.Rotherham: And, accepting a cup, giving thanks, he said—Take this, and divide among yourselves;

text.NRSV: for I tell you that from now on I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.”
text.Rotherham: For, I say unto you—In nowise shall I drink henceforth of the fruit of the vine, until, the kingdom of God, shall come.

text.NRSV: Then he took a loaf of bread, and after giving thanks he broke it and gave it to them, saying, “This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.”
text.Rotherham: And, taking a loaf, he gave thanks, and brake, and gave unto them, saying—This is my body [[which in your behalf is being given: this, be ye doing, in remembrance, of me.

text.NRSV: And he did the same with the cup after supper, saying, “This cup that is poured out for you is the new covenant in my blood.
text.Rotherham: And—the cup, in like manner, after the taking of supper, saying—This cup, is the new covenant in my blood, which, in your behalf, is to be poured out.]]

text.NRSV: But see, the one who betrays me is with me, and his hand is on the table.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, lo! the hand of him who is delivering me up, is with me upon the table.

text.NRSV: For the Son of Man is going as it has been determined, but woe to that one by whom he is betrayed!”
text.Rotherham: Because, the Son of Man, indeed, according to what is marked out, goeth his way; nevertheless, alas! for that man through whom he is being delivered up.

text.NRSV: Then they began to ask one another which of them it could be who would do this.
text.Rotherham: And, they, began to be enquiring together, among themselves, as to which, then, it could be, from among them, who, this thing, was about to perpetrate!

text.NRSV: A dispute also arose among them as to which one of them was to be regarded as the greatest.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that there was, a rivalry also, among them, as to which of them seemed to be greater [than the rest] .

text.NRSV: But he said to them, “The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them; and those in authority over them are called benefactors.
text.Rotherham: And, he, said unto them—The kings of the nations, lord it over them, and, they who wield authority over them, benefactors, are called.

text.NRSV: But not so with you; rather the greatest among you must become like the youngest, and the leader like one who serves.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, not so! On the contrary—the greater among you, Let him become as, the younger, and, he that governeth, as, he that ministereth,

text.NRSV: For who is greater, the one who is at the table or the one who serves? Is it not the one at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
text.Rotherham: For which is greater—he that reclineth? or he that ministereth? Is not he that reclineth? But, I, am, in your midst, as he that ministereth,

text.NRSV: “You are those who have stood by me in my trials;
text.Rotherham: Ye, however, are they who have remained throughout with me in my temptations;

text.NRSV: and I confer on you, just as my Father has conferred on me, a kingdom,
text.Rotherham: And, I, covenant unto you—as my Father hath covenanted unto me—a kingdom,

text.NRSV: so that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: That ye may eat and drink, at my table, in my kingdom, and sit upon thrones, judging, the twelve tribes of Israel.

text.NRSV: “Simon, Simon, listen! Satan has demanded to sift all of you like wheat,
text.Rotherham: Simon! Simon! lo! Satan, hath claimed you, to sift as wheat;

text.NRSV: but I have prayed for you that your own faith may not fail; and you, when once you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.”
text.Rotherham: But, I, have entreated for thee, that thy faith may not fail. And, thou, once thou hast turned, strengthen thy brethren.

text.NRSV: And he said to him, “Lord, I am ready to go with you to prison and to death!”
text.Rotherham: But, he, said unto him—Lord! with thee, am I, ready, both into prison and unto death, to be going.

text.NRSV: Jesus said, “I tell you, Peter, the cock will not crow this day, until you have denied three times that you know me.”
text.Rotherham: And, he, said: I tell thee, Peter—A cock will not crow this day, until, thrice, thou deny that thou knowest me.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “When I sent you out without a purse, bag, or sandals, did you lack anything?” They said, “No, nothing.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—When I sent you forth, without purse or satchel or sandals, of anything, came ye short? And, they, said—Of nothing!

text.NRSV: He said to them, “But now, the one who has a purse must take it, and likewise a bag. And the one who has no sword must sell his cloak and buy one.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—But, now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, in like manner also, a satchel; and, he that hath nothing, let him sell his mantle, and buy a sword!

text.NRSV: For I tell you, this scripture must be fulfilled in me, ‘And he was counted among the lawless’; and indeed what is written about me is being fulfilled.”
text.Rotherham: For I say unto you—This that is written, must needs be completed in me,—And, with lawless ones, was he reckoned; for, even that which concerneth me, hath, completion.

text.NRSV: They said, “Lord, look, here are two swords.” He replied, “It is enough.”
text.Rotherham: And, they, said—Lord, lo! two swords, here! And, he, said unto them—’Tis, enough!

text.NRSV: He came out and went, as was his custom, to the Mount of Olives; and the disciples followed him.
text.Rotherham: And going out, he went, according to his custom, unto the Mount of Olives; and the disciples [also] followed him.

text.NRSV: When he reached the place, he said to them, “Pray that you may not come into the time of trial.”
text.Rotherham: And, coming up to the place, he said unto them—Be praying, lest ye enter into temptation.

text.NRSV: Then he withdrew from them about a stone’s throw, knelt down, and prayed,
text.Rotherham: And, he, was torn from them about a stone’s throw; and, bending his knees, he began to pray,

text.NRSV: “Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me; yet, not my will but yours be done.”
text.Rotherham: saying—Father! if thou please, bear aside this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but thine, be accomplished.

text.NRSV: Then an angel from heaven appeared to him and gave him strength.
text.Rotherham: [[And there appeared unto him a messenger from heaven, strengthening him,—

text.NRSV: In his anguish he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.
text.Rotherham: and, coming to be in an agony, more intensely, was he praying; and his perspiration became, as if great drops of blood, falling upon the ground.]]

text.NRSV: When he got up from prayer, he came to the disciples and found them sleeping because of grief,
text.Rotherham: And, arising from prayer, he came unto his disciples, and found them falling asleep by reason of their grief;

text.NRSV: and he said to them, “Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray that you may not come into the time of trial.”
text.Rotherham: and he said unto them—Why are ye slumbering? Arise! and be praying, lest ye enter into temptation.

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking, suddenly a crowd came, and the one called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He drew near to Jesus to kiss him;
text.Rotherham: While yet he was speaking, lo! a multitude; and, he that was called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them on,—and he drew near unto Jesus, to kiss him.

text.NRSV: but Jesus said to him, “Judas, is it with a kiss that you are betraying the Son of Man?”
text.Rotherham: But, Jesus, said to him—Judas! with a kiss, the Son of Man, dost thou deliver up?

text.NRSV: When those who were around him saw what was coming, they asked, “Lord, should we strike with the sword?”
text.Rotherham: And they who were about him, seeing what would be, said—Lord! shall we smite with the sword?

text.NRSV: Then one of them struck the slave of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
text.Rotherham: And a certain one from among them smote, the High-priest’s, servant, and took off his right ear.

text.NRSV: But Jesus said, “No more of this!” And he touched his ear and healed him.
text.Rotherham: But Jesus, answering, said—Let be—as long as this! …And, touching the ear, he healed him.

text.NRSV: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come for him, “Have you come out with swords and clubs as if I were a robber?
text.Rotherham: And Jesus said unto the High-priests, and Captains of the temple, and Elders, who had come against him—As against a robber, have ye come out, with swords and clubs?

text.NRSV: When I was with you day after day in the temple, you did not lay hands on me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness!”
text.Rotherham: Daily, was I with you, in the temple, and yet ye stretched not forth your hands against me! …But, this, is your hour, and the authority of darkness.

text.NRSV: Then they seized him and led him away, bringing him into the high priest’s house. But Peter was following at a distance.
text.Rotherham: And, having arrested him, they led him away, and took him into the house of the High-priest; and, Peter, was following afar.

text.NRSV: When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
text.Rotherham: And, they having kindled up a fire in the midst of the court, and taken seats together, Peter was for sitting among them.

text.NRSV: Then a servant-girl, seeing him in the firelight, stared at him and said, “This man also was with him.”
text.Rotherham: But, a certain maid-servant, seeing him seating himself towards the light, and looking steadfastly at him, said—This one also, was, with him.

text.NRSV: But he denied it, saying, “Woman, I do not know him.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, denied, saying—I know him not, woman!

text.NRSV: A little later someone else, on seeing him, said, “You also are one of them.” But Peter said, “Man, I am not!”
text.Rotherham: And, after a little, another, beholding him, said—Thou also, art, from among them. But, Peter, said—Man! I am not.

text.NRSV: Then about an hour later still another kept insisting, “Surely this man also was with him; for he is a Galilean.”
text.Rotherham: And, about one hour having passed, a certain other, began stoutly to insist, saying—Of a truth, this one also, was with him; for indeed he is, a Galilaean.

text.NRSV: But Peter said, “Man, I do not know what you are talking about!” At that moment, while he was still speaking, the cock crowed.
text.Rotherham: But Peter said—Man! I know not what thou art saying! And, instantly while yet he was speaking, a cock crew.

text.NRSV: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said to him, “Before the cock crows today, you will deny me three times.”
